Position early and let the decade do the work. AI Infrastructure. The backbone everything runs on. The data centers, the compute, the owned infrastructure powering every model on earth. Every other sector on this list runs on top of it. Space. Connectivity and infrastructure moving off the planet and into orbit. Direct to phone networks, launch, and satellite constellations turning space into the next commercial frontier. Photonics. The plumbing that moves the data. As clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of GPUs, moving information at the speed of light becomes the constraint. A market tripling this decade as 800G goes to 1.6T goes to 3.2T. Nuclear Energy. The only power source that can actually feed AI at scale. Data centers need enormous, constant, carbon free baseload, and the grid cannot deliver it fast enough. Nuclear is the answer the entire industry is racing toward. Memory. The bottleneck nobody can build fast enough. Every AI server needs 8 to 10 times the memory of a normal one, the makers are sold out, and the shortage runs for years. No memory, no AI. Humanoid Robotics. AI stepping out of the screen and into the physical world. A market going from under $2 billion today to $38 billion by 2035. The companies making the brains, eyes, and sensors win no matter which robot wins. Quantum Computing. The frontier that breaks the limits of classical computing entirely. Still early, still small, but governments and the biggest tech companies on earth are pouring billions in. The most asymmetric bet of the group. Seven sectors. One direction. text:
CPO 연재 총정리 — 빛이 칩 안으로 들어오는 순간

6편에 걸친 CPO(Co-Packaged Optics) 연재의 요약본. 기술 핵심은 짧게, 투자 함의는 무게감 있게

핵심 메시지

1. CPO는 "되느냐 안 되느냐"의 기술이 아니라 "언제, 어디서부터 되느냐"의 기술이다.

2. AI 추론은 연산보다 데이터 이동에 전력을 더 많이 쓴다. ASIC 전력의 최대 절반이 칩 간 데이터 이동에 들어간다.

3. 구리는 전력벽(power wall)에 도달했다. 짧은 거리에서는 광학이 답이지만, 광학 부품도 전자 회로만큼 작아져야 그 이득을 실제로 누릴 수 있다.

4. Nvidia 로드맵은 2단계다. 2026년 Rubin(Spectrum6 스위치)에서 스케일아웃에 먼저 CPO 적용, 2028년 Feynman(NVLink8)에서 스케일업까지 확장.

5.같은 데이터를 보고도 Nvidia·Meta는 "이제 시작"이라 하고, Google·Broadcom은 "아직 아니다"라고 한다. 벤더 락인 우려와 2018년 온보드 옵틱스(OBO) 실패 경험이 후자의 신중론을 뒷받침한다.

기술 검증: 3가지 Kill Condition

6. 파이버 정렬 — 광파이버(8~10μm)와 실리콘 도파관(500nm급)을 50nm 오차 안에서 붙여야 한다. 일반 전기 정렬보다 100배 정밀해야 한다. 해법은 여러 개 동시에 검증되는 단계까지 왔지만, 표준이 하나로 수렴하지 않았다.

7. 레이저 신뢰성 — 생산된 레이저의 최대 50%가 스펙 미달이다. 업계는 ELSFP(레이저를 패키지 밖으로 분리)로 리스크를 줄이고, 중복화(Lightmatter)·다중화(Ranovus 퀀텀닷) 두 방향으로 신뢰성을 끌어올리는 중이다.

8. 테스트 데이터 통합 — 전기(STDF)와 광학(CSV/XLS) 검사 데이터가 서로 다른 포맷으로 따로 쌓인다. 전기 99%×광학 99% = 98% 수율로, 곱셈 구조가 양산 규모에서 손실을 키운다. 업계 중론은 표준화에 최소 2~3년 추가 소요.

9. 세 조건 중 가장 화려하지 않은 3번(테스트 데이터)이 CPO 전체 일정을 가장 오래 잡아끌 가능성이 높다.

투자 관점 — 누가 돈을 버는가

10. 파운드리 — $TSM: 패키지 안 모든 칩렛이 TSMC PDK·공정을 따라야 하는 구조적 락인. Nvidia·AMD·Google·AWS 가속기가 TSMC 패키징을 쓰는 한 광학 엔진 칩렛도 자동으로 TSMC산이 될 가능성. 다만 현재 실리콘 포토닉스 파운드리 시장은 연 10억 달러 미만으로 TSMC 매출의 1%도 안 됨 — 8배 성장 전망이 있지만 출발선 자체가 작다는 점은 균형 있게 봐야 함.

11. 레이저 — $COHR, $LITE: Coherent·Lumentum·Sumitomo 3사가 글로벌 레이저 시장 68% 점유. 레이저 시장은 2024년 200억 달러 돌파, 2030년 300억 달러 이상 전망. $NVDA가 두 회사에 각각 20억 달러 투자해 캐파 선점 — 공급이 수요를 못 따라가는 구간에서 자본이 먼저 줄을 선 신호. Lumentum 자체 추정 InP 광 레인 수요 CAGR 약 85%, 캐파 증설 속도가 계속 못 따라잡는 격차 존재.

12. OCS(광 회로 스위치) — 신흥 전장: CignalAI 추정 TAM 2028~2029년 연 30억 달러 이상. Google 외주화로 Lumentum·Coherent의 매출 기회가 몇 분기 만에 1억→4억 달러로 점프, 단일 고객 10억 달러 계약도 등장. Lumentum R300(MEMS 기반)이 검증된 강자, iPronics 같은 웨이브가이드(SiP) 신생 기업이 정면 도전 — CPO 본류보다 승자가 더 빨리 가려질 수 있는 영역.

13. 시장 규모 종합: pluggable 광모듈 시장 2023년 60억→2030년 250억 달러(Coherent), 광 컴포넌트 시장 2030년 250억 달러(OMDIA)/2029년 310억 달러(CignalAI), 패키징 포함 실리콘 포토닉스 IC 시장 2031~2034년 290억~320억 달러(마진율 50%+ 전망).

14. 인프라 자본 집약도: Ayar Labs·Wiwynn 시연 랙 기준 100kW 이상 전력 밀도, 100% 액체 냉각, HVDC 전력 아키텍처 필요. CPO 도입은 칩 설계 문제를 넘어 랙 전체 전력·냉각 인프라 교체를 요구하는 자본 집약적 사업.

15. 지정학 변수: 중국은 150mm 리튬나이오베이트 웨이퍼 자체 개발(CHIPX), 일본 257억 달러 투입, 인도 포토닉스 팹 최대 50% 자본 지원, 유럽은 M&A로 규모 확대 중. 인력 부족(2025년 상반기 R&D 공석 330개+)과 CEO 교체(IPG Photonics, Lumentum)는 업계 리더십 불안정성의 신호.

$TSM $COHR $LITE $NVDA 

매수, 매도 등 투자권유 아님.

Nvidia 로드맵은 2단계다. 2026년 Rubin(Spectrum6 스위치)에서 스케일아웃에 먼저 CPO 적용, 2028년 Feynman(NVLink8)에서 스케일업까지 확장. 5.같은 데이터를 보고도 Nvidia·Meta는 "이제 시작"이라 하고, Google·Broadcom은 "아직 아니다"라고 한다. 벤더 락인 우려와 2018년 온보드 옵틱스(OBO) 실패 경험이 후자의 신중론을 뒷받침한다. 기술 검증: 3가지 Kill Condition 6. 파이버 정렬 — 광파이버(8~10μm)와 실리콘 도파관(500nm급)을 50nm 오차 안에서 붙여야 한다. 일반 전기 정렬보다 100배 정밀해야 한다. 해법은 여러 개 동시에 검증되는 단계까지 왔지만, 표준이 하나로 수렴하지 않았다. 7. 레이저 신뢰성 — 생산된 레이저의 최대 50%가 스펙 미달이다. 업계는 ELSFP(레이저를 패키지 밖으로 분리)로 리스크를 줄이고, 중복화(Lightmatter)·다중화(Ranovus 퀀텀닷) 두 방향으로 신뢰성을 끌어올리는 중이다. 8. 테스트 데이터 통합 — 전기(STDF)와 광학(CSV/XLS) 검사 데이터가 서로 다른 포맷으로 따로 쌓인다. 전기 99%×광학 99% = 98% 수율로, 곱셈 구조가 양산 규모에서 손실을 키운다. 업계 중론은 표준화에 최소 2~3년 추가 소요. 9. 세 조건 중 가장 화려하지 않은 3번(테스트 데이터)이 CPO 전체 일정을 가장 오래 잡아끌 가능성이 높다. 투자 관점 — 누가 돈을 버는가 10. 파운드리 — $TSM: 패키지 안 모든 칩렛이 TSMC PDK·공정을 따라야 하는 구조적 락인. Nvidia·AMD·Google·AWS 가속기가 TSMC 패키징을 쓰는 한 광학 엔진 칩렛도 자동으로 TSMC산이 될 가능성. 다만 현재 실리콘 포토닉스 파운드리 시장은 연 10억 달러 미만으로 TSMC 매출의 1%도 안 됨 — 8배 성장 전망이 있지만 출발선 자체가 작다는 점은 균형 있게 봐야 함. 11. 레이저 — $COHR, $LITE: Coherent·Lumentum·Sumitomo 3사가 글로벌 레이저 시장 68% 점유. 레이저 시장은 2024년 200억 달러 돌파, 2030년 300억 달러 이상 전망. $NVDA가 두 회사에 각각 20억 달러 투자해 캐파 선점 — 공급이 수요를 못 따라가는 구간에서 자본이 먼저 줄을 선 신호. Lumentum 자체 추정 InP 광 레인 수요 CAGR 약 85%, 캐파 증설 속도가 계속 못 따라잡는 격차 존재. 12. OCS(광 회로 스위치) — 신흥 전장: CignalAI 추정 TAM 2028~2029년 연 30억 달러 이상. Google 외주화로 Lumentum·Coherent의 매출 기회가 몇 분기 만에 1억→4억 달러로 점프, 단일 고객 10억 달러 계약도 등장. Lumentum R300(MEMS 기반)이 검증된 강자, iPronics 같은 웨이브가이드(SiP) 신생 기업이 정면 도전 — CPO 본류보다 승자가 더 빨리 가려질 수 있는 영역. 13. 시장 규모 종합: pluggable 광모듈 시장 2023년 60억→2030년 250억 달러(Coherent), 광 컴포넌트 시장 2030년 250억 달러(OMDIA)/2029년 310억 달러(CignalAI), 패키징 포함 실리콘 포토닉스 IC 시장 2031~2034년 290억~320억 달러(마진율 50%+ 전망). 14. 인프라 자본 집약도: Ayar Labs·Wiwynn 시연 랙 기준 100kW 이상 전력 밀도, 100% 액체 냉각, HVDC 전력 아키텍처 필요. CPO 도입은 칩 설계 문제를 넘어 랙 전체 전력·냉각 인프라 교체를 요구하는 자본 집약적 사업. 15. 지정학 변수: 중국은 150mm 리튬나이오베이트 웨이퍼 자체 개발(CHIPX), 일본 257억 달러 투입, 인도 포토닉스 팹 최대 50% 자본 지원, 유럽은 M&A로 규모 확대 중. 인력 부족(2025년 상반기 R&D 공석 330개+)과 CEO 교체(IPG Photonics, Lumentum)는 업계 리더십 불안정성의 신호. $TSM $COHR $LITE $NVDA 매수, 매도 등 투자권유 아님."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2069927317850427532", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1074", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
It’s one thing to argue AI equities are in a bubble, but another to suggest the underlying buildout isn’t real.

The valuation argument may be correct, but it’s separate from the physical reality underneath.

Power, fabrication, photonics, and heat rejection remain scarce, capital-intensive, and difficult to scale.

Stocks can fall without changing what’s physically been built.

text: @manhdungYokiko are you claiming photonics is not profitable?
@SebKuhnert some accounts think there is only the photonics sector and tech sebastian
@Denis_13_1982 @aleabitoreddit its almost like there are sectors outside photonics to invest in.  Very strange.

This is super interesting from @MarvellTech 

Plasmonics era beckons

- Plasmonic-based Silicon Photonics light engines could support modules operating at 3.2T and beyond with lesser space and lower power per bit.

- SiPho modulators, while highly scalable and cost-efficient, are fundamentally constrained by the physical properties of light modulation in silicon, limiting their ability to scale efficiently to higher bandwidths.

- Plasmonic waveguides address these challenges by compressing light below the diffraction limit at a metal-dielectric interface. 

- These structures are integrated directly onto the silicon photonics platform, enabling the industry to build on the extensive foundation already established with SiPho while incorporating the performance advantages of plasmonics.

- Plasmonic waveguides consist of a metal-dielectric channel filled with an active Pockels material integrated into SiPho devices or other photonic material system. Thus, Plasmonic waveguides can respond much faster to electrical signals, enabling much higher modulation rates than conventional photonic modulators

- The technology is still in the early days of development for commercialization but lots of work required from scale manufacturing perspective.

$MRVL $LITE $COHR

I honestly think that $SIVE and $LPK sold out so hard today because @aleabitoreddit  posted favorably of memory and said photonics has been down and a bunch of lemmings went and dumped to go buy memory at the top.

It's probably better if we don't have such holders among us. 🤦‍♂️

Just seriously stupid and rash and I suspect lots who did it are not English speakers.
embedded_text:
$SIVE and $LPK butchered today on very low volume.

I'm guessing it's a rotation to memory after $MU's earnings, but nothing in the photonics and glass substrate thesis has changed. In fact there's indication it's moving along faster. And the memory ship has already sailed 🤷🏼‍♂️

On

text:
🧵 The Cosmic Virtual Metasurface: TSFTR-NF in the Lab
What if the universe isn’t a mechanical machine, but a software-defined reality?
A major breakthrough just published in Advanced Photonics Nexus by researchers at Nottingham Trent University didn't just revolutionize optics—it accidentally provided a real-world proof of concept for the Temporal‑Spatial Frame Theory of Reality (TSFTR‑NF).
Here is how cutting-edge laboratory photonics just validated a fractal network universe: 👇
1/ The Analog-to-Digital Shift
•In the Lab: Scientists discarded rigid, physical micro-lenses for "virtual metasurfaces." Using a Spatial Light Modulator (SLM), they project 2D digital patterns of light that update every 20ms, changing the lens's entire function on the fly.
•In TSFTR-NF: We discard smooth, continuous spacetime. Reality is a decentralized network of localized, discrete "frames" (Δt=tp ) that dynamically "render" and assemble themselves based on local causal interactions.
2/ 2D Holographic Projection
•In the Lab: The team used a flat, 2D emulated optical pattern to manipulate light in 3D space—focusing, steering, and changing its color concurrently without moving physical parts.
•In TSFTR-NF: Our framework relies on scale-dependent dimensionality. At the Planck scale, the spectral dimension (ds ) drops to 2. Spacetime emerges macroscopically because 2D quantum boundary threads weave together to project our 4D macro reality.
3/ Turning the Invisible Visible
•In the Lab: The breakthrough achievement was using a programmable light pattern to instantly convert invisible infrared wavelengths into visible light that a standard, cheap camera could capture, adjusting focal length on demand.
•In TSFTR-NF: This is a literal manifestation of our Reality Index R(r). By altering the local density of the network connections, the metasurface reshapes how information scales from boundary to volume. It proves that "matter" and "wavelengths" are just emergent properties of the underlying information network.
The Macro Conclusion
The Nottingham Trent team realized that to truly master light, you have to stop building rigid physical hardware and start programming the medium itself.
TSFTR-NF argues that nature figured this out at the Big Bang. The universe isn't a rigid clockwork canvas; it is a self-assembling, holographic fractal network. We are living inside a cosmic virtual metasurface.
The lab is finally catching up to the framework.
Shoutout to the global research teams pushing optics into the digital age! 📐✨

text:
🚨 השוק כבר לא קונה סיפורי AI.
הוא קונה את צוואר הבקבוק הבא.
בשנתיים האחרונות המשקיעים חיפשו את מודל ה-AI הבא.היום, השוק כבר מסתכל עמוק יותר.הוא מחפש את החברות שבלעדיהן אי אפשר יהיה להמשיך לבנות את מהפכת ה-AI.השבוע קיבלנו לכך חיזוק משמעותי.$MU הוכיחה שהביקוש לזיכרון AI ממשיך להיות חזק, עם חוזים ארוכי טווח שמחזקים את הוודאות העסקית.$QCOM הציגה אסטרטגיה ברורה להפוך משחקנית מובילה בסמארטפונים לחברה שמכוונת גם ל-Edge AI, מחשוב מתקדם ותשתיות AI.אבל מבחינתי...אלה לא החדשות המרכזיות.החדשות הן שהן מסמנות איפה נמצא צוואר הבקבוק הבא.בכל פעם שתעשיית ה-AI פותרת בעיה אחת...נוצרת בעיה חדשה.התקדמנו ממחסור בכוח מחשוב...למחסור בזיכרון.מזיכרון...לקישוריות.מקישוריות...לפוטוניקה.מפוטוניקה...לאריזות שבבים.ומשם...לציוד ייצור.לחשמל.לקירור.לדאטה סנטרים.ולבסוף...לחומרי הגלם והמינרלים הקריטיים שעליהם כל שרשרת הערך נשענת.לכן אני לא מחפש רק את מניית ה-AI הבאה.אני מחפש את החברות שפותרות את צוואר הבקבוק הבא.כי לשם, בדרך כלל, זורם ההון.כך אני מסתכל על השוק.לא דרך הכותרות.אלא דרך שרשרת הערך של ה-AI.מי שמבין איפה נוצר צוואר הבקבוק הבא, עשוי להבין גם איפה ייווצר הערך הבא.הטיקרים שאני עוקב אחריהם בתחום:
$MU $QCOM $NVDA $AVGO $MRVL $AMD $TSM $ASML $AMAT $LRCX $KLAC $WOLF $VRT $SMCI $ORCL $ANET $CRWV $ARM $MTSI $POET
#AI #ArtificialIntelligence #PhysicalAI #EdgeAI #Semiconductors #Memory #HBM #Photonics #DataCenters #Infrastructure #ChipStocks #TechStocks #StockMarket #Investing #WallStreet #LongTermInvesting #FutureTech #AIInfrastructure #ALMI #AviLeviTradesאין באמור המלצה לקנייה או למכירה של ניירות ערך. כל האמור הוא לצורכי לימוד והעשרה בלבד.

text:
$SIVE occupies a critical, highly leveraged position within the AI hardware ecosystem by architectural integration, serving as a vital enabler for the massive data streams generated by $MU advanced memory solutions. While $SIVE does not supply physical components directly into $MU High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) packages, its strategic collaborations with $MXL and optical innovators like Ayar Labs position it to capture outsized financial gains as Micron scales production.

In essence, $MU manufactures the hyper-fast data engines, while $SIVE provides the ultra-high-bandwidth optical and radio-frequency (RF) conduits required to keep those engines from bottlenecking.

Through its Sivers Wireless division, the company has established a deep co-development and reference design partnership with $MXL.

This collaboration merges $SIVE proprietary millimeter-wave (mmWave) RFIC transceivers with $MXL high-performance baseband modems to create unified, low-latency transmission platforms capable of exceeding 10 Gbps data rates.

When $MU secures record-breaking enterprise contracts for its HBM3E or HBM4 architectures, hyperscalers simultaneously scale their entire data center networking fabric to handle the massive influx of data.

$MXL captures this demand by deploying its specialized connectivity and storage accelerator platforms into the AI server racks.

Because $SIVE hardware is natively integrated into $MXL blueprints, every infrastructure win for MaxLinear triggers a direct cascade of high-volume component orders for Sivers, converting $MU macro-driven growth into tangible product revenue for the smaller chipmaker.

The most explosive long-term financial catalyst for $SIVE, however, lies in its Sivers Photonics division and its direct contribution to the optical computing revolution.

$MU next-generation memory layers process data at speeds that legacy copper wiring can no longer support without generating catastrophic thermal throttling and severe power degradation. $SIVE addresses this critical industry pain point by manufacturing advanced InP (Indium Phosphide) DFB laser arrays.

These specialized lasers act as the foundational light source for silicon photonics, transforming electronic data into high-speed light signals.

$SIVE feeds these laser arrays directly to Ayar Labs, an industry-standard optical interconnect pioneer backed heavily by $NVDA, $AMD, and $INTC, which integrates them into "TeraPHY" optical chiplets.

These chiplets sit directly alongside high-performance compute modules and memory stacks, allowing $MU HBM to communicate with GPUs at the speed of light.

Coupled with $SIVE strategic partnership with $GFS to embed these laser architectures directly into commercial AI foundry wafers, Sivers is transitioning from an engineering-services firm into a high-margin, high-volume hardware supplier. s.

Through its Sivers Wireless division, the company has established a deep co-development and reference design partnership with $MXL. This collaboration merges $SIVE proprietary millimeter-wave (mmWave) RFIC transceivers with $MXL high-performance baseband modems to create unified, low-latency transmission platforms capable of exceeding 10 Gbps data rates. When $MU secures record-breaking enterprise contracts for its HBM3E or HBM4 architectures, hyperscalers simultaneously scale their entire data center networking fabric to handle the massive influx of data. $MXL captures this demand by deploying its specialized connectivity and storage accelerator platforms into the AI server racks. Because $SIVE hardware is natively integrated into $MXL blueprints, every infrastructure win for MaxLinear triggers a direct cascade of high-volume component orders for Sivers, converting $MU macro-driven growth into tangible product revenue for the smaller chipmaker. The most explosive long-term financial catalyst for $SIVE, however, lies in its Sivers Photonics division and its direct contribution to the optical computing revolution. $MU next-generation memory layers process data at speeds that legacy copper wiring can no longer support without generating catastrophic thermal throttling and severe power degradation. $SIVE addresses this critical industry pain point by manufacturing advanced InP (Indium Phosphide) DFB laser arrays. text:
Elon Musk’s acquisition of Mesh puts the entire optical-interconnect sector on the strategic map.

Mesh builds the optical systems.

Sivers $SIVE develops the high-performance lasers and photonics technology that can power them. https://t.co/n7EwBJh4WN

Here is what Putin spoke:
"I am pleased to welcome the participants and organizers of the 13th Forum of Regions of Russia and Belarus, which traditionally takes place on the eve of Independence Day of the Republic of Belarus, celebrated on July 3 and marking the anniversary of Minsk's liberation from the Nazi invaders.

I sincerely congratulate all Belarusian friends on this national holiday. Russia will always remember that during the Great Patriotic War, our peoples, shoulder to shoulder and at enormous sacrifice, defended freedom and independence, bringing peace to Europe and all of humanity.

It is important that Russia and Belarus carefully preserve and enhance their fraternal traditions of friendship and mutual assistance, strengthening their historically established cultural and spiritual ties. Russian-Belarusian relations are truly strategic, and our Union State serves as a model of mutually beneficial and equal integration, effectively facilitating the deepening of cooperation within such associations as the Eurasian Economic Union, the Commonwealth of Independent States, and the Collective Security Treaty Organization.

One of the main driving forces behind the partnership between Russia and Belarus, as well as the construction of the Union State, are ties between regions. This is the solid foundation upon which cooperation between the two countries is steadily developing across a wide range of areas—political and security, social and humanitarian, and, of course, economic.

Forums of Regions regularly bring together representatives of federal and local authorities, legislators and businesspeople, experts, and public figures—in short, all those who help establish and develop direct contacts between Russian and Belarusian regions.

This year, more than 600 delegates from 73 constituent entities of the Russian Federation arrived in the capital of Belarus. Among them are governors, heads of regional legislative assemblies, deputies, ministers, and company executives. The Belarusian delegation is no less prestigious.

The substantial package of agreements and commercial contracts signed during the forum demonstrates our mutual focus on achieving concrete results.

You are discussing a truly relevant topic: "Economic Interaction between Regions – the Foundation for Sustainable Development of Russia and Belarus." I note that, thanks in large part to close interregional economic cooperation, bilateral trade turnover last year broke another record – $52 billion, according to our statistics. This year, growth continues – more than 7 percent in the first quarter. All mutual settlements between our countries are conducted in national currencies.

The harmonization of legislation in trade, investment, and financial regulation is actively underway. Approaches in customs and tax matters are being unified, and a unified quality control system for goods is being implemented. The Union State Committee on Standardization and Quality held its first meeting during the forum.

Russia leads the way in accumulated investment in the Belarusian economy, at approximately $4 billion. Companies with Russian capital operate in all regions of the republic, with approximately 2,500 of them nationwide.

The regions are closely involved in the implementation of 26 joint import substitution projects in high-tech sectors. For example, modern mining equipment is being manufactured with Belarusian partners in the Moscow Region, machine tool components are being produced in the Novosibirsk Region, and equipment from the Minsk Tractor Plant is being assembled in Tatarstan.

Production cooperation has been established between Belarusian civil aviation plants and the interregional cluster of the Irkutsk, Ulyanovsk, Nizhny Novgorod, and Voronezh Regions, which produces the MS-21 and Sukhoi Superjet 100 airliners. Design bureaus in the Sverdlovsk and Brest Regions are working on the Osvey light multirole aircraft project.

Leading Belarusian factories Integral and Planar supply components for electronics manufacturing in Tatarstan and the Nizhny Novgorod and Moscow regions. Companies in Moscow, St. Petersburg, and Minsk have established effective cooperation chains for the production of optical devices and photonics instruments for communications, data transmission, and IT systems."

Regular passenger flights operate from 10 Russian regions to Belarus. Cross-border commuter trains between Smolensk and Vitebsk and between Smolensk and Orsha were launched in April. Service between Velikiye Luki and Vitebsk, Velikiye Luki and Polotsk, and Bryansk and Gomel are next in line. Plans include the construction of a high-speed railway between Moscow and Minsk.

The railway and port infrastructure of Russian regions is being used to transport Belarusian exports. A permit-free transit procedure is being gradually introduced for Russian and Belarusian goods transported to third countries by road.

text:
Putin held a speech during their meeting:

"I am pleased to welcome the participants and organizers of the 13th Forum of Regions of Russia and Belarus, which traditionally takes place on the eve of Independence Day of the Republic of Belarus, celebrated on July 3 and marking the anniversary of Minsk's liberation from the Nazi invaders.

I sincerely congratulate all Belarusian friends on this national holiday. Russia will always remember that during the Great Patriotic War, our peoples, shoulder to shoulder and at enormous sacrifice, defended freedom and independence, bringing peace to Europe and all of humanity.

It is important that Russia and Belarus carefully preserve and enhance their fraternal traditions of friendship and mutual assistance, strengthening their historically established cultural and spiritual ties. Russian-Belarusian relations are truly strategic, and our Union State serves as a model of mutually beneficial and equal integration, effectively facilitating the deepening of cooperation within such associations as the Eurasian Economic Union, the Commonwealth of Independent States, and the Collective Security Treaty Organization.

One of the main driving forces behind the partnership between Russia and Belarus, as well as the construction of the Union State, are ties between regions. This is the solid foundation upon which cooperation between the two countries is steadily developing across a wide range of areas—political and security, social and humanitarian, and, of course, economic.

Forums of Regions regularly bring together representatives of federal and local authorities, legislators and businesspeople, experts, and public figures—in short, all those who help establish and develop direct contacts between Russian and Belarusian regions.

This year, more than 600 delegates from 73 constituent entities of the Russian Federation arrived in the capital of Belarus. Among them are governors, heads of regional legislative assemblies, deputies, ministers, and company executives. The Belarusian delegation is no less prestigious.

The substantial package of agreements and commercial contracts signed during the forum demonstrates our mutual focus on achieving concrete results.

You are discussing a truly relevant topic: "Economic Interaction between Regions – the Foundation for Sustainable Development of Russia and Belarus." I note that, thanks in large part to close interregional economic cooperation, bilateral trade turnover last year broke another record – $52 billion, according to our statistics. This year, growth continues – more than 7 percent in the first quarter. All mutual settlements between our countries are conducted in national currencies.

The harmonization of legislation in trade, investment, and financial regulation is actively underway. Approaches in customs and tax matters are being unified, and a unified quality control system for goods is being implemented. The Union State Committee on Standardization and Quality held its first meeting during the forum.

Russia leads the way in accumulated investment in the Belarusian economy, at approximately $4 billion. Companies with Russian capital operate in all regions of the republic, with approximately 2,500 of them nationwide.

The regions are closely involved in the implementation of 26 joint import substitution projects in high-tech sectors. For example, modern mining equipment is being manufactured with Belarusian partners in the Moscow Region, machine tool components are being produced in the Novosibirsk Region, and equipment from the Minsk Tractor Plant is being assembled in Tatarstan.

Production cooperation has been established between Belarusian civil aviation plants and the interregional cluster of the Irkutsk, Ulyanovsk, Nizhny Novgorod, and Voronezh Regions, which produces the MS-21 and Sukhoi Superjet 100 airliners. Design bureaus in the Sverdlovsk and Brest Regions are working on the Osvey light multirole aircraft project.

Leading Belarusian factories Integral and Planar supply components for electronics manufacturing in Tatarstan and the Nizhny Novgorod and Moscow regions. Companies in Moscow, St. Petersburg, and Minsk have established effective cooperation chains for the production of optical devices and photonics instruments for communications, data transmission, and IT systems.

Regular passenger flights operate from 10 Russian regions to Belarus. Cross-border commuter trains between Smolensk and Vitebsk and between Smolensk and Orsha were launched in April. Service between Velikiye Luki and Vitebsk, Velikiye Luki and Polotsk, and Bryansk and Gomel are next in line. Plans include the construction of a high-speed railway between Moscow and Minsk.

The railway and port infrastructure of Russian regions is being used to transport Belarusian exports. A permit-free transit procedure is being gradually introduced for Russian and Belarusian goods transported to third countries by road."

$SIVE   AI분석

1. $SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors) 핵심 사업과 최근 뉴스
Sivers Semiconductors는 스웨덴 회사로, 크게 두 축을 가지고 있습니다:
•  Photonics 부문: InP 기반 고출력·협대역 CW DFB 레이저와 레이저 어레이. AI 데이터센터 광연결(CPO), FMCW LiDAR에 특화.
•  Wireless 부문: mmWave 빔포머 IC (5G, Satcom, defense).
2026년 3월 발표된 LiDAR 뉴스가 정확히 사용자가 말한 내용입니다:
•  전략적 LiDAR 고객이 자사 기술을 플랫폼 전체에 채택.
•  2026년 Q4부터 생산 ramp 시작 (자동차 + 산업/로보틱스용).
•  공급 품목: CW DFB 레이저 + 광증폭기.
•  누적 수익 잠재력 $53M ~ $138M (고객 제품 수명 주기 기준).
이 고객은 공식 확인은 안 됐지만, 시장에서 Aeva로 강력하게 추정되고 있습니다. 이유:
•  Aeva는 FMCW LiDAR 전문 (협대역·고출력 레이저가 핵심).
•  Aeva + LG Innotek 전략 파트너십 (2025년 7월 발표, LG Innotek이 Aeva 모듈 제조·공급).
•  Aeva는 로보틱스·휴머노이드·산업 자동화도 타겟팅하고 있음 (Boston Dynamics Atlas 같은 고급 비전 시스템에 잘 맞는 기술).
이게 바로 사용자가 말한 “$AEVA와 LG Innotek을 통해 공급망으로 들어온다”는 부분입니다. 2026년 말 생산 확대 + 상당한 수익 잠재력도 정확히 맞아요.
2. $SPCX (SpaceX 프록시) Mesh Optical 인수와의 연결고리
최근 뉴스 (2026년 6월 26일경): Musk/SpaceX 측이 Mesh Optical Technologies 인수에 FTC 승인 받음. Mesh는 전 SpaceX 엔지니어들이 만든 스타트업으로, AI 데이터센터용 1.6Tbps 광 트랜시버를 개발 중입니다. DFB 레이저를 사용하고, flip-chip 패키징 등 고효율 광 인터커넥트 기술.
연결 포인트:
•  Mesh가 필요로 하는 핵심 부품 중 하나가 고성능 DFB 레이저 → Sivers가 정확히 만드는 제품.
•  Sivers는 이미 Ayar Labs, POET Technologies 등 CPO(코패키지드 옵틱스) 플레이어에 공급 중.
•  SpaceX/Starlink 생태계(레이저 기반 위성 간 통신) + xAI/Tesla 데이터센터 수요 증가 → 광·포토닉스 전반 수요 확대.
•  X(트위터)에서도 최근 “Mesh 인수 후 SIVE가 레이저 공급으로 수혜”라는 언급이 나오고 있어요.
결론: LiDAR 뉴스 자체가 Mesh 인수와 “직접” 연결되는 건 아니지만, 전체 포토닉스/AI 인프라/Satcom 테마에서 시너지 효과가 분명히 있습니다. $SIVE가 “포토닉스 + LiDAR + Satcom” 3박자 비대칭 플레이인 건 맞아요.
3. 주가 상황과 투자 포인트
•  52주 범위: 2.85 SEK ~ 110 SEK (고점 6월 2일).
•  현재 (6월 26~27일경): 약 63 SEK 수준 → 고점 대비 약 42~43% 하락.
•  YTD +1,400% 이상, 1년 +1,500% 이상 폭등 후 조정. “2000% 올랐으니 기다리자” 하던 사람들 입장에서는 정확히 조정 구간.
강점 (Bull case):
•  AI 데이터센터 광연결(CPO) + 로보틱스 LiDAR + Satcom 수요가 동시에 터질 수 있는 희귀한 포지션.
•  Opportunity pipeline 이미 $799M (2025년 말 대비 +77%).
•  기술적으로 InP DFB 레이저에서 경쟁력 있음 (특히 narrow linewidth + high power).
리스크 (Bear case, 상당히 큼):
•  극단적 고평가: trailing P/S 60x 이상. 많은 분석가들이 “과대평가”라고 지적.
•  아직 적자 지속 (Q1 2026 revenue SEK 61.9M, 전년比 -22%, 손실 확대). 실행 리스크 높음.
•  고객 집중 리스크, ramp 지연 가능성.
•  과거 희석 이력 있음. 소형주(시총 약 18~19B SEK)라 변동성 극심.
•  경쟁사: Coherent, Lumentum 등 대형 플레이어 존재.
종합 의견
$SIVE에 극도로 bullish할 만한 근거는 충분합니다. 특히:
•  Aeva/LG Innotek 공급망 + 2026년 Q4 ramp
•  Mesh Optical 인수로 인한 포토닉스 테마 강화
•  AI + 로보틱스 + Satcom 3중 수혜 가능성
고점 대비 40% 조정한 지금이 “겁먹고 포기할 타이밍”은 아니에요. 다만 고위험 speculative 종목이라는 점을 명확히 해야 합니다. valuation이 이미 많은 성공을 선반영하고 있어서, ramp가 늦어지거나 기대가 어긋나면 추가 조정도 충분히 나올 수 있어요.
지금 사야 할까?
→ 장기적으로 photonics/AI 인프라를 강하게 믿고, 변동성을 감당할 수 있다면 분할 매수 관점에서 관심 가질 만한 구간입니다.
→ 단기 트레이딩이나 “무조건 오른다” 마인드라면 리스크가 매우 큽니다.

text:
$SIVE is down here not because of anything Sivers did. 

The entire photonics complex is in a drawdown, and that's exactly the kind of dislocation worth leaning into.

What actually happened:
On June 9, @SemiAnalysis_ published an institutional-only note flagging potential delays in CPO deployment, with a yield-based bear argument (their math: 95% per-engine yield compounded across 32 optical engines = ~19% system yield). 

The note hit institutional desks first. The whole optical group sold off together: $AAOI -14%, $COHR -11%, $POET -12%, $LITE -8%, $MRVL -7.6%, and $SIVE -8.7% alongside them.

Here's the tell. Under the exact same negative news, $AAOI fell ~17% intraday while Lumentum, with a highly overlapping technology profile, fell ~8%. Roughly a 2x difference in decline for two names exposed to the identical headline. 

That spread is the signature of a positioning-driven shakeout flushing out the loosest hands (high retail concentration, high margin balances), not a calm fundamental repricing. 

The names that bled most were the ones with the loosest float, not the ones with the worst fundamentals.

This hit a sector that was up triple digits YTD. $AAOI +390%, $MRVL +228%, $LITE +138%, $COHR +107%, $POET +100%. $SOXX dropped while the $VIX barely moved. That combination, sharp single-name drawdowns with no systemic panic, is profit-taking and sector rotation off parabolic runs, not a demand collapse.

And on the other side of the same week: $NVDA's own blog states Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics is now in full production, with CPO-based next-gen Spectrum-X switching going into scale-out and scale-across deployments on the Vera Rubin platform. 

The demand signal didn't break. A timing-and-magnitude call on the ramp got mistranslated by the time it reached retail into "CPO is cancelled," which it isn't.

A CPO timing delay does not threaten the Sivers thesis the way it threatens a name priced for imminent volume.

Sivers isn't trading on 2026 CPO revenue. The CPO ramp was always a 2027 qualification, 2028 volume story. 

Even @aleabitoreddit, asked directly if the selloff shook it's faith in photonics, said they're more bullish as prices drop.

When a quality supply-chain position gets sold purely because it shares a sector ETF with the names institutions are trimming, that's the dislocation you want. 

Buying the bottleneck while the complex flushes weak hands is a better entry than chasing it green. 

Just size it as the high-beta position it is, because the volatility that created this entry cuts both ways.

If you are still bullish on $SIVE - drop a like!

Not Financial Advice. DYOR.

text:
The $QQQ as launched in March of 1999.  The ETFs always arrive at exactly the wrong time, enabling the masses to plow into the most bloated, overvalued themes with ease and at little cost.  

How does this look for AI?  You were early in mid 2023 and 2024.  Then in 2025 you were in the middle innings.  And now you are late anon, with many launches per month popping up in AI and related themes.  

Everyone is in, and everyone is an expert. 

Dozens of AI-related ETFs have launched in the past 3 years (roughly mid-2023 to mid-2026), contributing to a total of over 90 AI-themed ETFs now available in the US. 
Key Context on Launches
•  Pre-2023 baseline: A smaller number existed (e.g., broader robotics/AI funds like BOTZ or AIQ from earlier years). The post-ChatGPT AI boom (late 2022 onward) triggered a sharp increase in new, targeted launches. 
•  Notable recent examples:
•  2023: Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT, launched May 2023) — one of the first pure generative AI-focused funds. 
•  2024–2026: A wave including iShares A.I. Innovation and Tech Active ETF (BAI, Oct 2024), various Global X, Invesco, WisdomTree, First Trust, and niche products (e.g., memory/semiconductor photonics, pure-play AI infrastructure like DRAM in 2026). Multiple launches per month in peak periods, often active or thematic.

text:
Robotics has been spoke about a lot this weekend. Certainly feels like the algo is pushing it in my direction.

Initially when $OUST was getting pushed in my direction months ago, it appeared to be one of the many tickers that circulates X due to group speak.

The fact it’s circulating my feed again now though.. months later, intrigues me.

Look, physical AI is the next frontier of the AI buildout. It will be the next wave.

Do I think it is incredibly early? Yes.

But early often is where the highest level of alpha is found.

Once I’ve completed researching into photonics / opticals/ connectivity I will be delving into the perspective of physical AI.

Before I start diving into individual companies I need to understand thoroughly the entire sector and when the sector is likely to move as a whole.

Do you own any stocks currently within the robotics trade? $OUST so far is the main ticker I see circulating X and of course $TSLA but this is more so product not the component required to put together the product.

text:
$SIVE  found a strategic edge hidden within O-NET : 3SP Technologies, a French specialty foundry in Nozay that now locks down the photonic AI supply chain.

This is not just a commercial partnership; it is a full-stack vertical integration strategy. O-NET serves as the industrializing force that integrates Sivers' high-performance laser IP into one of the most robust photonics solutions on the market. 

By plugging directly into this end-to-end industrial chain, Sivers is positioned in the optimal place to capture the surging demand for AI infrastructure, having solved the industry's hardest challenge: scaling photonics from the lab to the data center.

The winning equation for next-generation architecture:
$SIVE  (Design): High-performance DFB laser array architecture.

3SP Technologies (French Fab): Specialty foundry mastering III-V material epitaxy (InP).

O-NET (Scale): High-volume industrial assembly of ELS (External Light Source) modules for CPO (Co-Packaged Optics).

Why this matters for Hyperscalers:

O-NET does not just distribute Sivers; they package Sivers' designs into proven, industrial-grade modules, effectively bridging the gap between design and mass production. For Hyperscalers, this is about risk mitigation and supply chain sovereignty. 

By controlling the design (Sivers), the laser core fabrication (3SP), and high-density packaging (O-NET), this trio delivers a de-risked, "ready-to-deploy" solution for the 2027 infrastructure cycle, bypassing traditional bottlenecks. Vertical integration is no longer a luxury; it is the industry standard.

text:
totally agree on "IMO Photonics is the next supercycle but we’re closer to the beginning, a lot of the major revenue ramps begin mid 2027."

The market has already started to price in the theme, yet many of the real revenue ramps are not expected to begin until mid-2027 and beyond. That means the story is less about chasing what has already happened, and more about identifying which parts of the photonics stack will convert today’s technical bottlenecks into revenue over the next several years.

For me, the first places to look have always been the prerequisites for that ramp. Before photonics can become a true revenue supercycle, the industry needs enough manufacturing capacity and enough test infrastructure to support it. That is why I have focused heavily on two layers of the stack: fabs such as $TSEM, $GFS, $TSM, $XFAB, and $UMC, and measurement/testing names such as $FORM, $TER, $VIAV, $ATEYY, $KEYS, and $AEHR.

Of course, the broader opportunity also includes assembly, packaging, substrates, lasers, and many other layers. But in my view, fabs and testing are the two early gates. Without them, the ramp cannot scale; with them, today’s technical demand can begin turning into real, visible revenue.

The market has already started to price in the theme, yet many of the real revenue ramps are not expected to begin until mid-2027 and beyond. That means the story is less about chasing what has already happened, and more about identifying which parts of the photonics stack will convert today’s technical bottlenecks into revenue over the next several years. For me, the first places to look have always been the prerequisites for that ramp. Before photonics can become a true revenue supercycle, the industry needs enough manufacturing capacity and enough test infrastructure to support it. That is why I have focused heavily on two layers of the stack: fabs such as $TSEM, $GFS, $TSM, $XFAB, and $UMC, and measurement/testing names such as $FORM, $TER, $VIAV, $ATEYY, $KEYS, and $AEHR. Of course, the broader opportunity also includes assembly, packaging, substrates, lasers, and many other layers. But in my view, fabs and testing are the two early gates. text:
$POET now officially connected to a top 3 hyperscaler via Lumilens. 

Be patient and know what you own. $POET is by far my current biggest bet in photonics /  AI infrastructure theme and I have been in photonics for over 2 years now. 

I wouldnt be shocked if we surpass $AAOI in marketcap within the next 18 months.

text:
Here is one attempt to map the ⏳progress around co-packaged optics (CPO) from 2️⃣0️⃣2️⃣2️⃣ through expected developments in 2️⃣0️⃣2️⃣7️⃣.
Over the years, several organizations have developed CPO solutions, including Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Intel, ROCKLEY PHOTONICS LIMITED and IBM. However, Broadcom’s 25.6T CPO demo in 2022 helped bring renewed attention to the topic.
The timeline shows how major players are approaching the space differently: NVIDIA pushing toward micro ring modulator-based CPO switch availability, Broadcom advancing from early demos to 102.4T delivery, Marvell Technology linking CPO with custom XPUs, and RANOVUS Inc. targeting ASIC/XPU integration.
What is also important is the broader ecosystem effort behind CPO. System-level requirements and interoperability standards are shaping up. 
Personally, I am curious to see how this advanced technology develops beyond a few large corporations, and howa broader ecosystem can grow around CPO. 
❤️Constructive feedback is welcome ❤️

text:
Absolutely. Washington can fund the science, but quantum won’t scale as a collection of separate sectors:

Cryogenics. Control electronics. Photonics. Vacuum. Packaging. Foundry capacity.

They’re one physical stack, so scaling requires industrializing all of it.

Follow the bottlenecks all the way down to where value accrues.

According to Straits Times, Singapore is key to GlobalFoundries' quest to AI. 

That's because Temasek sold homegrown Chartered Semiconductor to GF and this Singapore fab has since become the most productive for GF.

Also, GF continues to acquire local firms since. Advanced Micro Foundry (AMF) a Singapore Silicon photonics firm in 2025 and possibly an unnamed local semiconductor firm recently.

Some of the FinX favorites YTD.

$SOFI down 35% 
$ASTS down 1%
$NOW down 39%
$ZETA down 10%
$HOOD down 9%
$ONDS down 11%

At one point. I owned all 6 of these. I sold 5 of them, for gains, some small some more. Now I’m down to 1. $SOFI 

I still believe that all these companies will do well and their stock will go up eventually. I might even buy back at some point. 

BUT

I’m also not blind to the opportunity in front of me, thats been happening all year.

AI Infrastructure
Semis
Memory
Photonics
To name a few

Yes having conviction is very important, but having an open mind works too. 

$DRAM this etf alone might beat 95% of all individual stocks this year.

🟥 ⛏️ 🔎 Blue Amber is a rare and captivating mineraloid, not a true mineral, formed from ancient tree resin that fluoresces a deep blue under sunlight.
YT 🎞 Please visit: @olunrockhoplapidary
🔁 ❤️ Greatly Appreciated                                                                 

Found primarily in the Dominican Republic.

It dates back to the Miocene Epoch, roughly 15 to 20 million years ago, preserving botanical inclusions and volatile compounds that reveal the tropical ecosystems of its time.

Unlike mineral crystals, this mineraloid originates from the hardened resin of prehistoric Hymenaea trees, its blue fluorescence caused by aromatic hydrocarbons that interact with sunlight.

When viewed under ultraviolet light, Blue Amber glows with an ethereal blue fire, a natural optical effect that distinguishes it from golden or honey ambers.

Its rarity and organic origin make it a geological and chemical marvel, bridging paleobotany and photonics in one radiant specimen

This phenomenon makes Blue Amber a window into prehistoric ecosystems, preserving botanical chemistry from millions of years ago.

Its luminous glow and organic origin remind us that even fossilized resin can hold the living pulse of Earth’s history.
#BlueAmber

text:
👀some anons tried telling you what it was 10 years ago and they laugh...

“Kraken” — DOE’s monster cryogenic quantum platform
Kraken is the nickname for one of the world’s largest dilution refrigerators, built by Bluefors and installed at Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL).

“The Seven Dwarfs” — the seven DOE Quantum Testbeds
This is the part most people online misunderstand.
Inside the U.S. Department of Energy’s quantum program, the “Seven Dwarfs” is an informal nickname for the seven major national quantum testbeds distributed across DOE labs.

Snow White & Seven Dwarfs

🧠 The “Seven Dwarfs” — DOE Quantum Testbeds (List Format)
1. Sleepy — Argonne National Laboratory (Q‑NEXT)

2. Grumpy — Sandia National Laboratories (QSCOUT ion‑trap testbed)

3. Doc — Fermilab (SQMS superconducting quantum materials center)

4. Happy — Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (Berkeley Quantum Testbed)

5. Bashful — Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL quantum sensing + photonics)

6. Sneezy — Los Alamos National Laboratory (LANL quantum simulation + annealing)

7. Dopey — Oak Ridge National Laboratory (QSC + Kraken dilution refrigerator + Frontier integration)

Everyone forgot about Odin...

text:
$SIVE quietly has a consumer sensing business almost nobody is modeling. Their own product page spells it out.

→ DFB lasers for "biometric and health monitoring"

→ Target customers: "OEMs and device manufacturers developing optical sensing modules for wearables, AR/VR systems, health monitors, and smart home devices"

→ Output power up to 100mW across 1200-2400nm wavelengths

→ Ultra-low FIT rates (<1) — the reliability bar consumer electronics demand

→ Optimized for hybrid silicon photonics integration
Why these specs matter:

The 1200-2400nm range is the band used for optical biosensing through skin — the exact wavelengths relevant to non-invasive health monitoring like blood glucose and blood oxygen. The sub-1 FIT rate is the failure tolerance a company shipping tens of millions of consumer units would require.

Now connect it to what the company has said:

→ CEO Vathulya: "two technologies feeding three supercycles"

→ Management has repeatedly flagged sensing and biosensing as optionality the market isn't pricing

Here's the discipline point: Sivers names no customers on this page. None. So anyone telling you exactly who's buying is guessing.

What's NOT a guess: $SIVE has a productized, consumer-grade, health-sensing laser line, built to the reliability spec that mass-market wearables require, sitting inside a company the market is valuing almost entirely on its AI datacenter story.

The wearables/biosensing leg is real. It's just not priced.

$SIVE

text:
$LASE 
Laser Photonics'te CFO Değişimi

Lazer teknolojileri üreticisi Laser Photonics, Roman Franklin ile yollarını ayırdı ve geçici CFO (Acting CFO) olarak Ralph Venegas'ı atadı.

Hisse %2,84 düşüşle $1,71 seviyesine gerilerken, bu yönetimsel değişimin şirketin operasyonel maliyetlerine ve mali disiplinine etkisini takip etmek gerekir.

Son günlerde 2 önemli yönetici değişimi 🤔

text: Dokładnie tak — z dobrym autopilotem (ArduPilot/iNav), solidnym montażem i dobrze skalibrowanym systemem da się wyciągnąć zaskakująco dużo nawet z przeciętnych sensorów MEMS. Wibracje, czas lotu i prędkość rzeczywiście mocno wpływają na dryf.Ale tu właśnie jest różnica.ANELLO X3 nie jest ulepszonym MEMS-em. To optyczny żyroskop (technologia SiPhOG – silicon photonics), który działa na zasadzie efektu Sagnaca, a nie wibrującej mechanicznej struktury.  Dzięki temu ma znacznie lepszą stabilność biasu (< 0,5°/h) i jest dużo mniej podatny na wibracje, temperaturę i przyspieszenia niż nawet dobre taktyczne MEMS-y.  To właśnie dlatego Amerykanie (i sektor obronny) w to wchodzą — bo chcą mieć wysoką precyzję inercji w małym, tanim i masowo produkowanym urządzeniu, a nie tylko „dobre wyniki przy idealnych warunkach i krótkim locie”.Z X3 dryf rośnie znacznie wolniej, więc przy dłuższym locie bez GPS (albo po utracie obrazu) różnica robi się już bardzo wyraźna — szczególnie przy RTH, w rojach czy przy misjach na 10–20+ km.Czyli nie chodzi o to, że „wcześniej było tragicznie”. Chodzi o to, że teraz da się to zrobić znacznie lepiej i taniej niż klasycznymi wojskowymi FOG-ami. Inaczej jaki sens byłby w ogóle w to wchodzić...

text:
$IONQ acquired Skyloom primarily for full-stack ownership and quantum-specific integration. 

Quantum networking & entanglement focus: 
Skyloom’s free-space optical tech (OCTs) provides the photonic layer for distributed quantum entanglement, quantum key distribution (QKD), and ultra-secure communications. 

IonQ combines it with its quantum computing, ID Quantique (crypto), Vector Atomic (timing/sensing), and Capella (SAR satellites) assets to build an end-to-end quantum-secure space network. 

Mynaric terminals (standard SDA-compatible laser links) excel at classical high-bandwidth data but lack native design for quantum state preservation or entanglement distribution.

Vertical integration & control:
Owning Skyloom gives IonQ full IP, customization, and supply-chain control for future satellites (transition announced for 2027+). Relying on Mynaric (now owned by competitor Rocket Lab) creates dependency risks. Skyloom was already SDA-qualified with strong delivery history (e.g., Tranche 1 terminals).

Performance: 
IonQ highlights potential boosts like up to 500% higher throughput and dramatically lower latency (hours → <1 hour) when paired with their ecosystem. Skyloom emphasizes secure, high-capacity LEO-LEO/LEO-ground links optimized for quantum-secure routing and defense applications.

Skyloom vs. Mynaric: 
•  Mynaric (CONDOR Mk3 etc.) — Proven, SDA-certified, used on Acadia-10 for 2.5 Gbps demos. Strong for classical SAR data relay and general OISL. Now scaled under Rocket Lab.
•  Skyloom — Also SDA-certified/interoperable. Focuses on high-volume production, on-orbit routing, and integration with quantum/secure systems. Both meet similar SDA standards; differences are in optimization (e.g., Skyloom for quantum protocols, lower SWaP in some designs, or specific defense features).

Bottom line: For a pure commercial SAR latency fix, Mynaric worked fine (as on Acadia-10). IonQ’s strategy is building a quantum internet / secure space backbone, where owning a tailored optical layer (Skyloom) + quantum hardware gives a differentiated, controllable platform.

Capella Space validated a Rocket Lab Mynaric optical communications terminal on its newest satellite — the first time Capella has deployed an optical terminal. 

Capella Space released the first synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images from its Acadia-10 satellite on Wednesday, after the satellite launched in March. 

The satellite is currently demonstrating data transfer at 2.5 gigabits per second in onboard tests, Capella reported, enabling the satellite to downlink data more quickly, avoiding the wait for the satellite to make contact with a ground station.

“This near-real-time handoff bypasses the multi-hour ground station wait. The impact is profound; task-to-delivery timelines compress from hours down to minutes, fundamentally changing how organizations operationalize SAR data,” the company said in a blog post. 

Capella first announced plans to put Mynaric OCTs onboard its satellites back in 2021. But future Acadia satellites will have Skyloom OCTs instead, a Capella spokesperson told Via Satellite.  Both Capella and Skyloom are now owned by quantum company IonQ after acquisitions. 

Both acquisitions tie in with IonQ’s goal to bring quantum networking to space. Capella satellites are set to be equipped with Skyloom OCT’s starting in 2027, the spokesperson said. 

The terminal on Acadia-10 is compatible with the Space Development Agency (SDA) OCT standard to communicate with the SDA’s proliferated Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) network. Capella has been working with the SDA since 2021. In April of this year, the SDA awarded Capella an award under HALO Europa Track 1 in April to design two satellites to demonstrate “advanced tactical waveform performance, adaptive beamforming, and secure tactical communications in LEO.”

text:
$BLG IS AN ORBITAL DC ENABLER: 

The Mesh acquisition, the NVIDIA silicon photonics switch, Cerebras WSE-3 — are proof positives of the fact that the underlying physics of intra-satellite links was never the real constraint. 

Capital + Talent = Solution.

$AXE.AX $DVL.AX $WBT.AX

$BLG IS AN ORBITAL DC ENABLER: 

The intra-satellite link bottleneck is more tractable: photons travel at the speed of light, silicon can modulate light at terahertz frequencies, and fibre bandwidth scales with wavelength division multiplexing. 

$AXE.AX $DVL.AX $WBT.AX

$SPCX $SIVE

text:
Does this recent news about $SIVE not seem to fit perfectly with $SPCX's latest purchase of Mesh?

With their specialized InP DFB lasers for FMCW LiDAR

Which are essential for advanced vision systems in humanoids like Boston Dynamics' Atlas

This comes through the supply chain with $AEVA and LG Innotek

With production ramps scheduled for late 2026 and significant revenue potential

$SIVE is an asymmetric play across its three branches

Photonics, LiDAR, and Satcom alike

Impossible to be bearish on $SIVE

Especially with the discount it offers right now

I've been reading for weeks "I'll wait for it to correct a bit, it's up 2000%, I'm not going to chase the price"

Now it dropped 40% from highs

Now is when you have to buy, are you going to chicken out?

Extremely bullish on $SIVE

text:
Hey @elonmusk after the move for Mesh Optical Technologies, there may be another company worth putting on the SpaceX radar:

Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE

Mesh can help build the optical network.

Sivers develops the lasers and beamforming chips that could help power it.

That matters because Sivers is not only exposed to AI optical interconnects. The company also develops:

✅ High-performance CW DFB lasers and laser arrays
✅ Optical technologies for AI and high-performance computing
✅ Ka-band beamforming ICs
✅ Technology for electronically steered satellite terminals
✅ Resilient multi-orbit communications

https://t.co/1SfidBr02o awarded Sivers an $8.2 million production order for multi-beam Ka-band beamforming ICs intended for next-generation tactical satellite terminals, with volume production planned through 2027.

That means Sivers already operates at the intersection of three areas that are increasingly strategic to your ecosystem:

AI infrastructure.
Optical networking.
Satellite communications.

For SpaceX and Starlink, a partnership could strengthen access to critical semiconductor technology.

A strategic investment could align future product development.

An acquisition could bring both advanced photonics and Ka-band beamforming IP directly into the ecosystem.

Mesh may be part of the optical network.

Sivers could become part of the technology that powers the light and connects it to space.

Worth a serious look, Elon 🙏🏻

$SPCX $SIVE 🇺🇸🤝🇸🇪

text:
I can identify three pockets of strength today.

Space is the most obvious.

The narrative is unchanged; a new cold war. China replaces the USSR, same goal, total spatial dominance.

That requires massive infrastructure, paid by government grants, and those grants are accelerating.

More launches - $RKLB $SPCX
More payloads - $LUNR $FLY
More hardware - $RDW
More services - $ASTS $SPIR $BKSY $PL

The first leg is over. We're in the first real, violent retest for most of these names. This is max opportunity.

Leaders confirmed. Market loves the narrative. Prices deflated.

Energy is the second.

Most names - especially the 800VDC narrative, are just starting their first leg. Not yet confirmed fundamentally, but odds are in our favor. The higher up the chain, the better.

GaN and SiC - $NVTS $POWI $TXN
Solid-State Transformers - $SEDG $ENPH $ETN
Bus and PoL - $MPWR $IFX
Heat management - $VRT $MOD
Volatility management - $FLNC

AI hardware is the third, and it runs through several layers.

GPUs and CPUs are already sky-high and crowded. The opportunity left is in the technologies that optimize compute.

Memory, not buyable here - $MU $SNDK
Connectivity, buyable on retests - $SILC $ALAB $MRVL $ANET
Photonics, crowded but starting to breathe - $AXTI $IQE $SOI $COHR $LITE

Three verticals, three bull runs, different stages.

All these names will go higher because analysts can't model their market and revenue growth fast enough. They've been constantly wrong and will continue to be, which is our opportunity. Those names are basically my daily watchlist - and some more.

They aren't just narratives. They're structural shifts in our world.

text:
Did you miss the semi stock boom? ❌
Did you miss the memory stock boom? ❌
Did you miss the photonics stock boom? ❌

Physical AI / robotics is ramping. 🟢

Most of us are already aware of the large cap exposure in the space ( $NVDA , $MU , $IBM , etc) but here are 10 lesser-known, higher-concentration names where the robot buildout IS the story — not a side bet. 

Smaller, thinner, more violent. Speculative by construction. NFA 🧵👇

$SYM — Symbotic. Warehouse automation at real scale; ~$22B backlog vs a $432B in-house TAM.
Bear case: Walmart concentration — lose the anchor, lose 50%+ regardless of tech.

$SERV — Serve Robotics. Sidewalk + hospital bots (Diligent), ~2,000 deployed, Q1 rev +578% YoY.
Bear case: ~$49M loss on $3M rev, ~29% short interest — burn is doing parkour through the income statement.

$OUST — Ouster. Digital lidar, the 3D "eyes" robots need. Q1 rev $49M +49% YoY, Stereolabs adds AI vision.
Bear case: lidar is competitive + commoditizing; needs autonomy volume to justify the multiple.

$VPG — Vishay Precision Group. Force/torque + precision sensors — the #2 supply bottleneck after actuators. Q1 $84M, B2B 1.21.
Bear case: legacy industrial cyclicality; robotics is still a small slice of the mix today.

$AMBA — Ambarella. Low-power vision SoCs — the perception silicon at the edge.
Bear case: still tied to ADAS/IoT cycles; robotics is optionality, not yet the revenue driver.

$RR — Richtech Robotics. Service/hospitality bots, expanding into conversational AI.
Bear case: tiny rev base, lottery-ticket profile, triple-digit "targets" = risk dressed as upside.

$FFAI — Faraday Future. Pivoting to "physical AI"; EAI robot line, 2026 target 1,500 units.
Bear case: dilution-heavy legacy EV story bolting on a robot narrative — highest show-me factor here.

$KSCP — Knightscope. Autonomous security robots — a rare US deployed-fleet pure play.
Bear case: micro-cap, reverse-split history, persistent dilution.

$PATH — UiPath. Agentic automation — the software brain orchestrating physical + digital workflows.
Bear case: agentic competition is brutal; "physical AI" link is still more thesis than revenue.

$NNDM — Nano Dimension. Additive mfg / electronics printing — compresses the build/prototype cycle.
Bear case: indirect, turnaround-dependent, no clean robotics revenue line yet.

The honest meta-bear: mostly unprofitable, thinly covered, single-customer or single-product names. This is a watchlist, not a back-up-the-truck list. Entry / stop / size defined BEFORE the trade — every time.

NFA.

"Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNzA5NDA1NDI2OTYxNDQ4OTk=", "text": "Did you miss the semi stock boom? ❌ Did you miss the memory stock boom? ❌ Did you miss the photonics stock boom? ❌ Physical AI / robotics is ramping. 🟢 Most of us are already aware of the large cap exposure in the space ( $NVDA , $MU , $IBM , etc) but here are 10 lesser-known, higher-concentration names where the robot buildout IS the story — not a side bet. Smaller, thinner, more violent. Speculative by construction. NFA 🧵👇 $SYM — Symbotic. Warehouse automation at real scale; ~$22B backlog vs a $432B in-house TAM. Bear case: Walmart concentration — lose the anchor, lose 50%+ regardless of tech. $SERV — Serve Robotics. Sidewalk + hospital bots (Diligent), ~2,000 deployed, Q1 rev +578% YoY. Bear case: ~$49M loss on $3M rev, ~29% short interest — burn is doing parkour through the income statement. $OUST — Ouster. Digital lidar, the 3D "eyes" robots need. Q1 rev $49M +49% YoY, Stereolabs adds AI vision. Bear case: lidar is competitive + commoditizing; needs autonomy volume to justify the multiple. $VPG — Vishay Precision Group. Force/torque + precision sensors — the #2 supply bottleneck after actuators. Q1 $84M, B2B 1.21. Bear case: legacy industrial cyclicality; robotics is still a small slice of the mix today. $AMBA — Ambarella. Low-power vision SoCs — the perception silicon at the edge. Bear case: still tied to ADAS/IoT cycles; robotics is optionality, not yet the revenue driver. $RR — Richtech Robotics. Service/hospitality bots, expanding into conversational AI. Bear case: tiny rev base, lottery-ticket profile, triple-digit "targets" = risk dressed as upside. $FFAI — Faraday Future. Pivoting to "physical AI"; EAI robot line, 2026 target 1,500 units. Bear case: dilution-heavy legacy EV story bolting on a robot narrative — highest show-me factor here. $KSCP — Knightscope. Autonomous security robots — a rare US deployed-fleet pure play. Bear case: micro-cap, reverse-split history, persistent dilution. $PATH — UiPath. Agentic automation — the software brain orchestrating physical + digital workflows. Bear case: agentic competition is brutal; "physical AI" link is still more thesis than revenue. $NNDM — Nano Dimension. Additive mfg / electronics printing — compresses the build/prototype cycle. Bear case: indirect, turnaround-dependent, no clean robotics revenue line yet. The honest meta-bear: mostly unprofitable, thinly covered, single-customer or single-product names. This is a watchlist, not a back-up-the-truck list. Entry / stop / size defined BEFORE the trade — every time. text:
Japan just crushed 90%+ of light's quantum noise — a world-record 10.1 dB squeeze on a chip.

The fuel for a room-temperature optical quantum computer Japan wants running at 10,000 qubits by 2027. It all started with a 1998 teleportation gamble.

#QuantumComputing #Photonics https://t.co/OUf7yK19yn

I call them BAG7, have been saying this for many months. 
Follow the liquidity, capital flowing from MAG7 to capex receivers -> semi equipment, memory, photonics, compute, energy for data center. 

Investors ask” Is it not cheap enough , how can they sell MSFT and META??”
Well for institutions, every dollar invested has to compound and that is unlikely with dilution and debt to raise capital. 
One question every investor should ask- What are we expecting from MAG7 to change their profitability story while this situation persists? 
If you don’t have an answer, that’s your answer.

text:
The ~22% sell-off on $AAOI in the past month definitely hurts.

But I still like them even at $11.1B MC.

Especially as they target ~$471M monthly transceiver revenue.

Which is annual rev of ~$5.6B = prices $AAOI at just ~2x fwd sales.

Ofc you'd want the ramp to actually deliver off the back of their ongoing capacity expansions for 800G/1.6T units. 

At which point, there'd be a ~9x inflection in monthly capacity when you compare Q1 2026 to End of 2027 projections.

And that whole Texas capacity expansion is still ongoing w/ ClassOne Technology just announcing orders from $AAOI for multiple wet processing systems.

So still early, as expected.

Naturally things have gotten a bit quiet lately on the news-front given they're mid-qualification from their hyperscale customer.

When they received a new $71M follow-on order back in April from them which >2x'd their backlog for that single customer.

Just important to bear in mind that it's still early in the photonics cycle too.

With firms like Yole projecting SiPh and Optical Interconnect markets to grow at 30% and 20% CAGR through to 2030.

text:
Silicon on Insulator-based #CMOS manufacturing and #SiliconPhotonics manufacturing often start from a similar wafer structure: Top silicon || Buried oxide || Silicon handle wafer
But the purpose of each layer is very different.
👉🏻In SOI CMOS, the top silicon is the transistor body.
👉🏻In #siliconphotonics SOI, the top silicon forms the optical waveguide core.
The top silicon for #siliconphotonics SOI wafer is typically much thicker than in FD-SOI, ranging from more than 150 nm to several microns depending on the platform. Around 220 nm is commonly used as a pseudo-standard in many silicon photonics processes (There is an interesting story about how 220nm became a psudo-standard - that is for another time 😊). The BOX is also much thicker because it must optically isolate the guided mode from the silicon substrate.
So the same basic wafer stack supports very different physics:
👉🏻In CMOS SOI, thickness variation affects electrical behavior:
 threshold voltage, leakage, hysteresis, floating-body effects, self-heating, and short-channel control.
👉🏻In #siliconphotonics SOI, thickness variation affects optical behavior:
effective index, phase, resonance wavelength, coupling efficiency, and insertion loss, etc.
💡One useful way to think about wafer thickness impact on silicon photonics device performance: 
Per nanometer of geometry error, waveguide thickness usually has a stronger impact (1.5x)  on optical properties than waveguide width. But in real fabrication, waveguide width variation often dominates the total spread because width errors are usually much larger than SOI thickness errors.
Same SOI stack.  Different specs.  Different physics.  Different process-control priorities.

"text": "Silicon on Insulator-based #CMOS manufacturing and #SiliconPhotonics manufacturing often start from a similar wafer structure: Top silicon || Buried oxide || Silicon handle wafer But the purpose of each layer is very different. 👉🏻In SOI CMOS, the top silicon is the transistor body. 👉🏻In #siliconphotonics SOI, the top silicon forms the optical waveguide core. The top silicon for #siliconphotonics SOI wafer is typically much thicker than in FD-SOI, ranging from more than 150 nm to several microns depending on the platform. Around 220 nm is commonly used as a pseudo-standard in many silicon photonics processes (There is an interesting story about how 220nm became a psudo-standard - that is for another time 😊). The BOX is also much thicker because it must optically isolate the guided mode from the silicon substrate. So the same basic wafer stack supports very different physics: 👉🏻In CMOS SOI, thickness variation affects electrical behavior: threshold voltage, leakage, hysteresis, floating-body effects, self-heating, and short-channel control. 👉🏻In #siliconphotonics SOI, thickness variation affects optical behavior: effective index, phase, resonance wavelength, coupling efficiency, and insertion loss, etc. 💡One useful way to think about wafer thickness impact on silicon photonics device performance: Per nanometer of geometry error, waveguide thickness usually has a stronger impact (1.5x) on optical properties than waveguide width. $KIOXIA is officialy my third big concentrated position after $SIVE and $NBIS.

Sold three positions today.
- $PENG (winner)
- $BRUN (breakeven)
- $MU (winner)

Bought more $KIOXIA. Think this is going to $500-600B by next year. Probably more after that. Projected revenue growth is huge, check it out.

Read that again, just so you don't miss out on that information afterwards.

Clarification: 
- $PENG, $MU and $BRUN are all longs too, in my view. The reason for selling was to add more into $KIOXIA and other current concentrated positions that are at discounts.
- Conviction hasn't changed just because I sold. I'll definitely look to re-enter into $BRUN & $PENG soon. 
- Don't like to overdiversify into more than 2-3 holdings in a sector. Won't stop coverage on any. Especially $BRUN that I think is the next $CRWV with much better financials.

Holdings:
- Photonics: $SIVE $AAOI
- Backend tests: $MIRAE $TRT 
- Memory: $KIOXIA $MACRONIX 
- AI cloud: $NBIS
- Swings: $KEEL (cloud), $SMCI (AI servers)

Playing three sectors today. Photonics, Memory & AI clouds. Watching Space ($RKLB, $ASTS, $PL) and Chips ($CBRS, $QCOM) closely. 👀

Will send out % later on. Much love fam!

text:
scritto parecchio su #Sivers in Svezia, ma in mano a due indiani, che mi sembra una truffa dopo che l'hanno pompata da 5 a 110 corone
 (ho anche scritto a della gente che la pompa ("gli indiani ti fotteranno"). Oggi -20%

21 giugno 2026
Menziona Sivers tra le “mode del momento” insieme a Lite e Coherent ($COHR) per le connessioni fotoniche.
Poi fa un lungo spiegone sul perché il packaging avanzato è diventato cruciale con l’AI (HBM vicino al processore, chiplet, banda, consumi energetici). Cita BESI come pure-play preferito del settore.
Sivers viene citata solo di passaggio come esempio di trend fotonico. 

10 giugno 2026
Riferisce al video Nvidia sul CPO con Lambda e scrive:  “ho scritto un sacco di post su questi ‘photonics’ cioè connessioni basate sulla luce o CPO che sono l’ultima moda della speculazione, tipo $Sivers, $Lite, Coherent $COHR”.
Cita poi il blog ufficiale Nvidia sul risparmio energetico e sui problemi delle connessioni tradizionali. 

4 giugno 2026 (due post)  Sul forte oscillamento del titolo:  “#Sivers comunque oscilla del 20% in giù in poche ore… ci sono più tweets su Sivers che su Nvidia o Google o Micron… è incredibile la valanga di post complicati che imbambolano la gente su una società che deve ancora produrre ricavi in questo mitico ‘CPO’ dei datacenters”.  

Altro post:  “Sivers in Svezia è diretta da due dell’India – uno ha venduto tutte le sue azioni il 31 maggio incassando 100 milioni di corone… Sivers perde circa 30-40 milioni anche nel 2026”. 

4 giugno 2026
Post breve e diretto:  “the indians at Sivers are going to screw you” 

3 giugno 2026
Sul rialzo dopo l’annuncio GlobalFoundries:  “#Sivers è tornato su a bomba perché hanno annunciato che saranno fornitori di GlobalFoundries e gli influencer hanno messo dozzine di post gasatissimi… In euro vale 10 euro e 2,8 miliardi, il target degli analisti resta 0,9 euro perché perde 56 milioni…”.
Sottolinea che i ricavi arriveranno tra un anno e che ci sono già altri fornitori. 

Post precedenti (1-3 giugno)
Continuano sulla stessa linea:  Annuncio GF è solo una qualifica/reference design, non un ordine con numeri (non cambia i fondamentali 2026).  
Sivers è solo uno dei tanti fornitori in piattaforme altrui (es. via POET).  
Perdite alte, cassa limitata, restatement dei conti, rischio diluizione.  
CEO indiano e stock che sale forte per poi crollare (da 3 a 88 a 61 corone).

text:
Daily Market Alpha Recap

We scanned 39 YouTube videos, 21 Reddit posts/threads, 1,709 X posts, and 4 newsletters since the last update.

The market conversation moved beyond “Micron beat.” The more important read is what the memory shock is doing to the rest of the stack: hardware prices, hyperscaler margins, power demand, and AI infrastructure positioning.

1. Memory scarcity is now showing up in end-product pricing

Apple and Microsoft became major parts of the feed after reports of price hikes across Macs, iPads, Home devices and Xbox hardware.

That matters because it validates the supplier thesis but creates pressure elsewhere. $MU, $SNDK, $WDC, $000660.KS and $005930.KS benefit from tight memory supply. But $AAPL, $MSFT and other hardware/platform companies now have to decide whether to absorb higher component costs or pass them to consumers.

The market liked the memory winners. It was less comfortable with the companies paying for that memory.

2. The cleaner institutional trade is semis vs hyperscalers

Bloomberg framed the trade clearly: own the companies getting paid by AI capex and be more careful with the companies funding it.

This is why semis and memory held the narrative while hyperscalers looked weaker.

Long side:
$SMH, $MU, $QCOM, memory/storage, equipment, construction and power beneficiaries.

Pressure side:
$AMZN, $GOOGL, $MSFT, $META, where higher memory, compute, power and infrastructure costs can hit free cash flow.

3. Natural gas entered the AI infrastructure debate

MacroVoices added one of the more differentiated ideas: AI buildout is becoming a power bottleneck, and natural gas is the practical bridge fuel.

The preferred expression was $UNL over $UNG because UNG has more front-month roll/contango drag. They also discussed Dec 2026 natural gas futures for convex upside.

This is a cleaner way to play AI power demand than simply buying every data-center utility.

4. Qualcomm became an option-value AI infrastructure story

$QCOM discussion picked up after investor day and analyst revisions.

The bull case is that Qualcomm is moving from handset/baseband exposure into data-center AI infrastructure, with management targeting material revenue from custom silicon and inference chips.

The bear case is that this business is still unproven.

That disagreement is the setup. $QCOM is now being debated as an AI infrastructure option, not just a mature mobile chip stock.

5. Oil risk premium faded, but Hormuz tail risk is not dead

Earlier today, oil was about relief: Hormuz reopening, Brent giving back war gains, and lower crude helping airlines/utilities.

In the last update, the story became more two-sided. X posts flagged safe-passage restrictions, possible ship-strike investigation, and Iranian route-control language.

The read:
the full war premium is gone, but the tail risk remains.

Lower oil helps airlines, utilities and Asia importers. But the cleaner energy discussion today was still natural gas/power, not crude.

6. Market structure looked weaker under the surface

QTR argued that the Nasdaq giving up a strong Micron-led open quickly was a bad signal. Reddit also discussed pension-fund de-risking: overfunded pensions rotating out of growth equities and into bonds.

This fits the tape:
semis strong, hyperscalers weak, $QQQ fragile, bonds bid.

Possible expression:
long selected semis / capex beneficiaries, underweight $QQQ, or watch $TLT if de-risking flows continue.

7. Photonics got more specific

Gaetano’s Substack narrowed the optical trade to 6-inch InP capacity and yield.

The point was not “optics good.” The point was that whoever can scale high-yield 6-inch InP wins the next leg of AI optical infrastructure.

Best-positioned in that note:
$COHR

Later / more challenged:
$LITE, $AAOI

This matters because the photonics trade is moving from theme exposure to manufacturing execution.

8. BTC wrappers stayed fragile

Crypto remained weak. $BTC had ETF outflow and liquidation pressure. $MSTR and $STRC kept trading like capital-structure stress rather than clean BTC exposure.

The strongest negative thesis was on $STRC: if the marginal buyer now requires distressed-style returns, the security may need to reprice lower unless the structure stabilizes.

Clean read:
if bullish crypto, own clean assets. Be careful with wrappers.

9. Smaller single-name pockets

$BB: QNX re-rating thesis gained attention. The stock is being reframed from dead phone/meme stock into embedded OS exposure for software-defined vehicles and physical AI.

$FCEL: data-center fuel-cell thesis kept building around the 380MW supply agreement, Fairfax zoning, and Exxon/carbon-capture angle.

$SONY: Substack argued Sony is the better GTA VI toll-booth trade than $TTWO because it captures platform fees, PSN subscriptions and microtransaction economics without owning game-development risk.

$HYPE: strongest crypto-specific long chatter outside BTC, supported by fee revenue and Multicoin valuation framework.

Bottom line

The update was about the second-order effects of memory scarcity.

The market is starting to separate:
who benefits from higher AI input costs,
who pays those costs,
who can pass them through,
and who has real infrastructure bottleneck exposure.

Best areas:
memory/storage, selected semis, AI power/natural gas, $QCOM optionality, $COHR/6-inch InP, $BB/QNX, selected data-center power names.

Most vulnerable:
hyperscalers facing rising input costs, $AAPL/$MSFT hardware margin pressure, $QQQ if de-risking continues, $MSTR/$STRC wrappers, and crowded AI names without near-term cash-flow support.

text:
3. Infrastructure (AI Factories, Networking, Cloud)
The data centers, high-speed networking fabrics, cooling, and orchestration layers that turn individual chips into giant intelligent systems.
Retail edge: Networking and connectivity specialists (e.g., Broadcom, Arista, and related silicon photonics or connectivity names) often sit in a sweet spot — critical to scaling clusters but sometimes less crowded than pure GPU names. Data center power delivery and thermal management plays can also offer better entry points for patient capital.

text:
Jensen Huang is investing in every photonics company he can find and the reason why tells you everything about where AI is headed (Save this). 

Lip-Bu Tan, the CEO of Intel says, when he looks for investment opportunities, he looks for the bottleneck and right now, the bottleneck is the interconnect, the pipes that move data between chips inside an AI data center.

That is why he backed Credo Semiconductor, Astera Labs and Celestial AI on the optical side.

Here is the simple version of what the interconnect bottleneck actually means.

Think of an AI data center like a city, the GPUs are the buildings where all the work happens but for those buildings to function, you need roads connecting them,  fast roads that can carry enormous traffic without congestion. 

And those roads are now the single biggest constraint on AI performance.

As clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of GPUs, traditional copper wiring is hitting its physical limits and that is where this entire sector comes in.

Credo Semiconductor (CRDO) is the most direct pure play on this theme, Credo makes high speed cables and optical chips that connect GPUs inside data center racks. 

Their revenue tripled in fiscal 2026 to $1.3 billion, growing 272% year over year at its peak and four of the world's largest hyperscalers each individually account for more than 10% of Credo's revenue.

Astera Labs (ALAB) solves the connection problem between different chip types.

Astera makes the PCIe and connectivity chips that manage data flow between GPUs, CPUs, and memory without errors or slowdowns. 

Their revenue grew 93% year over year to $308 million in Q1 2026 alone.

The optical companies are where the longer-term and potentially larger opportunity lives.

Copper has physical limits, you can only push electrical signals so far before the signal degrades, the heat spikes and power consumption explodes. 

The solution is light, fiber optic connections that move data using photons instead of electrons which is faster, cooler and far more energy efficient.

Jensen Huang made this clear at Computex 2026 because copper works as long as physically possible but at greater distances and larger scale, optics takes over. 

Coherent (COHR) is the most established optical company in this space.

Coherent makes the lasers, transceivers, and optical components at the foundation of all fiber optic communications. 

Nvidia signed a multibillion-dollar purchase commitment and invested $2 billion directly into the company and their customer order books are already extending out to 2028.

Marvell (MRVL) is the most comprehensive bet across the entire connectivity stack.

Marvell makes chips for optical networking, PCIe switching and custom AI silicon. 

Jensen Huang called Marvell the next trillion dollar company at Computex 2026 and backed it with a $2 billion Nvidia investment.  

Marvell also acquired Celestial AI, the exact company Lip-Bu Tan backed for $3.25 billion, gaining photonic fabric technology delivering 16 terabits per second of bandwidth.

Lumentum (LITE), Corning (GLW), and Ciena (CIEN) round out the major public names.

Lumentum received a $2 billion Nvidia investment for laser and photonics components.  

Corning known mostly for phone glass received $500 million from Nvidia for optical connectivity work and is up over 100% year to date.  

Ciena runs the optical networking systems between data centers and is seeing analyst price targets raised on the back of the AI optics boom.

Every time a hyperscaler spends a billion dollars on Nvidia GPUs, the surrounding infrastructure, cables, switches, transceivers, optical components has to be upgraded to match. 

The smarter the GPU gets, the more the interconnect matters.

Nvidia has committed at least $6.5 billion to photonics companies in the past 4 months alone and the companies building the roads between the GPUs may end up being just as valuable as the companies building the GPUs themselves.

Follow me @MelvinInvests for more AI, semis and the next big market themes.

text:
Am I crazy for thinking that $MU and $SIVE are going to rise until the US market opens?

Afterwards, I think that slight drops could occur until July due to macro

$SIVE showed bounce strength recovering 17% from the bottom

With the acquisition of Mesh by $SPCX, do you think photonics could take a breather? At least stay flat

On the other hand, $MU fell on Friday, it has everything blowing in its favor, the same as $SIVE

Slight breakout rise or flat?
