text:
NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang at Coherent, Sherman TX:

"AI runs on compute, but it scales with connectivity"

everyone's watching the GPU race - Jensen says the real bottleneck is something else entirely

→ connecting millions of chips across a data center requires light, not electricity. copper can't travel hundreds of feet fast enough. photonics - converting electricity to laser pulses - is what makes AI factories possible at scale
→ the full AI stack has five layers: energy grid, chips, infrastructure, models, applications. a breakthrough at any layer changes the economics of all the others
→ AI could be what finally makes sustainable energy fully market-driven - no subsidies needed for the first time in history. nuclear, solar, wind - all fundable by AI demand alone

bookmark this ↓

→ the scary scenario https://t.co/AqqK6oBeRj

3. Infrastructure (AI Factories, Networking, Cloud)
The data centers, high-speed networking fabrics, cooling, and orchestration layers that turn individual chips into giant intelligent systems.
Retail edge: Networking and connectivity specialists (e.g., Broadcom, Arista, and related silicon photonics or connectivity names) often sit in a sweet spot — critical to scaling clusters but sometimes less crowded than pure GPU names. Data center power delivery and thermal management plays can also offer better entry points for patient capital.

text:
Our fren @FundaAI posted in May about the 2.4T (300G lanes) adoption timelines. I found it very important, and I took good note.

Now professor Damnang is hearing the same in the street about it (remember he has contact with the main players in the Industry).

The higher technology bar needed in Photonics for 2.4T , in my view, will benefit the Big Players $LITE $COHR $AAOI etc. vs Tier 3 suppliers

Next big question is.... will delay fully CPO adoption? 🤔

https://t.co/MSW5Xft2Wp
embedded_text:
One interesting thing I have been hearing in the market recently is that before moving directly from 1.6T to 3.2T, the industry may take an intermediate step through 2.4T.

In other words, the market may not immediately push lane speed to 400G.

text:
🚨The best deep dive I've seen on the optical chokepoint. Worth reading in full.

The core point is the one I keep coming back to → the constraint in AI optics isn't the GPU or the silicon photonics die. It's the III-V InP laser that silicon physically cannot produce, made in a handful of specialized fabs with 18 month lead times and qualification lock-in measured in years. Own the layer they're forced to buy. 🧐

How I'm positioned across the photonics stack:

🔵 $SIVEF → the merchant InP laser itself. The scarcest node, design-ins across Ayar, $POET $GFS ✅
🟠 $AAOI → the vertically integrated US player making its own lasers and modules end to end. ✅
🟠 $CRDO → one layer over → the connectivity and retiming that the optical lanes run through. (Copper → silicon opticals in-house with Dust Photonics acquisition)

The laser is the bottleneck. The stack around it is the trade. ⚡️

Thanks for sharing Nexus Finance work @StormDirac 

Long $SIVEF $AAOI $CRDO (3 of my largest holdings) 💪

Anders, this is one of the best breakdowns of the laser bottleneck. The framing that silicon is the highway and InP is the headlights gets at the thing most photonics coverage misses → the chokepoint isn't the silicon chip everyone models, it's the III-V laser they can't make on it. 🧐

The M&A-as-validation read is on point too. Watching the acquirers tells you where the locked-in revenue lands two years out. Appreciate the depth here, this is the kind of work that actually moves the conversation forward. I am RTing it for all! Long $SIVE $SIVEF and $AAOI (2 of my largest positions!) 🤝

text:
$SIVE AGM & Q1 2026 – The board upgrade that signals M&A readiness right now
 I’ve been reflecting on the AGM outcome and the Q1 transcript, and one thread stands out more clearly than before: this company is deliberately positioning itself for the next layer of acceleration.
 Vickram was direct about it:

“This brings a very experienced and balanced board composition for us, and in particular, tremendous executive credibility and experience with U.S. markets, as well as strong M&A experience as we look not only at organically growing our business, but also adjacencies, complementary portfolios as well for the future if that can help further accelerate growth for the company.”

That’s not just some corporate line. That’s the CEO openly saying they’re keeping the door open for smart acquisitions.

The new board members feel spot on for this phase. Joakim Nideborn as Vice Chairman with his listed-company CFO experience and Helena Svancar with her deep M&A background (including time as Head of M&A at $ERIC) these are exactly the kind of people you want around the table when you’re thinking about scaling faster than organic growth alone can take you.

I’ve seen this story play out with $SIVEF before. Back in 2017 they acquired CST Global, what became Sivers Photonics. It was a bold bet on III-V lasers and the Glasgow foundry. There was dilution, integration headaches, and it took several years before the payoff became obvious. But look where we are today: that same platform is right in the middle of the AI data center laser ramps, LiDAR production ($AEVA), and the $JBL pluggable collaboration. It completely transformed what $SIVE could do.

MixComm followed a similar path, added real wireless depth and U.S. muscle, and now it’s helping drive the SATCOM momentum with https://t.co/A7mlfHMHLp / $YSS and the big IRIS² opportunity in Europe.
Both deals took time and patience, but they delivered real acceleration. I have a strong feeling we’re heading for something similar again. The setup just feels right: a nearly $800M pipeline that’s still growing fast, multiple 2027 ramps stacking up, and now a board with genuine M&A firepower. I’m actively digging into potential candidates right now, and when I’ve vetted something properly I’ll share my thoughts.
 What makes this moment particularly interesting is how the transcript frames the bigger picture. Vickram described being in the middle of three super cycles at once AI factories, space/SATCOM, and modern warfare and noted that $SIVE has differentiated technology that can meaningfully support elements of all three. He also highlighted the IRIS² European satellite infrastructure effort as a substantial long-term opportunity for the wireless business: a pipeline north of $80 million through 2030 (and that’s not even half the lifetime revenue potential), with $SIVE already engaged with five subcontractors in the final RFP stage for user terminals that start development in 2027. As an EU-based provider, they’re uniquely positioned on the sovereignty angle.
 The board changes, the explicit openness to adjacencies and M&A, the progress on U.S. dual-listing readiness, and the manufacturing capacity build-out all point in the same direction: $SIVE is building the foundation and the optionality to grow both organically and through smart additions at exactly the moment the secular tailwinds are strongest.
 I remain deeply convicted as a long-term shareholder. The transformation from development-led to product-led is progressing, the pipeline is a genuine lead indicator, and the company now has the governance and expertise in place to act on opportunities that can meaningfully accelerate the trajectory.
 Small volumes turning into bigger numbers and potentially even bigger ones, faster, with the right moves.

What are your thoughts? Did anything in the transcript or the board changes stand out to you? Or any areas you think would make sense for a potential acquisition? Would love to hear!

text:
@FonteauO You clearly know this field much better than I do. 
Your point about wafer-scale, photonics and new cooling approaches makes sense. I agree cooling will probably change a lot. I’m just not sure heat as a constraint ever completely disappears and therfore instability. but ❤️
@FonteauO Using light (photonics) does not eliminate cooling requirements. But hopefully u r right

@ArtofSpecuycky Spot on. Optical interconnects (COHR, MRVL) and advanced packaging (AMKR) are the ultimate L0/L1 chokepoints. As GPU clusters scale, photonics isn't just a cycle; it's the physical bottleneck dictating L2 datacenter limits. Are you tracking glass substrates here?…

text:
My thesis was that stocks top with $SPCX.

Why?

Stock bros up 20x, crypto bros buying stock bros bags at ATH’s; the very same “retail” that was crushed at the top of every crypto bull cycle…

Everyone following stock KOL’s like @aleabitoreddit and ape whatever ticker is mentioned.

Trillion dollar IPO’s.

Literally the same sh** we’ve seen at the top of every crypto bull cycle.

Of course, the difference is these are real companies with real revenue, but timing does still matter unless you want to sit -30% under water for a couple years and HODL.

And at the end of the day, businesses fail, competition is fierce, there’s no way every single one of your bottleneck photonics glass substrate hydro-cooling picks are going to just keep going up over time.

Everyone’s a genius season- notice it when it’s there and start looking for the exit.

text:
$SIVE / $SIVEF Bull Case: Why Sivers Semiconductors could become the next $LITE

(Disclaimer-I have a position in Sivers)

Everyone wants the next GPU winner.

Few are looking at the companies enabling AI infrastructure behind the scenes.

That’s the Sivers thesis.

The opportunity isn’t becoming another Nvidia—it’s becoming part of the connectivity layer that AI depends on.

As AI clusters scale, compute is no longer the only bottleneck. Moving massive amounts of data efficiently is becoming just as important. More bandwidth, lower latency, lower power.

That’s where optical networking and photonics enter.

Sivers is positioning around:
• Optical engines
• External laser solutions
• Co-packaged optics (CPO)
• AI datacenter connectivity

The reason bulls compare it to $LITE:

Lumentum became valuable because optical components quietly became mission critical.

If AI infrastructure follows a similar path, the companies enabling data movement—not just compute—could see outsized value creation.

Sivers has also built relationships across the ecosystem through collaborations and development efforts with major manufacturing and photonics players. That doesn’t guarantee success—but it creates a path to relevance.

The bet here isn’t that Sivers dominates AI.

The bet is that it captures a small but important piece of a market growing exponentially.

Small company.
Big TAM.
Execution required.

That’s where asymmetric setups come from.

text:
If you're not buying today there's probably no reason to be checking your portfolio every five minutes
Close the app and go outside

It's always refreshing being around people in the real world who have absolutely no idea the market is down

I don't think this is the start of a bear market or some epic crash. If anything, the market seems to be getting a little ahead of itself on rate cut expectations

$MU reports tonight and I think it'll be one of the more important reads for the AI and photonics trade

Volatility is the price you pay for superior returns
It sucks but that's the game we play

text:
The following is an objective and comprehensive analysis of $NOK, $MRVL, and $MSFT, based on the latest publicly available financial reports and reliable sources (as of around June 24, 2026; the most recent quarter for NOK/MRVL/MSFT is their respective FY period; some Q2/Q3 updates are available).

1. $NOK (Nokia Corporation)

Company Overview: A global leader in telecommunications network infrastructure, with businesses covering mobile networks (5G/6G), optical networks, IP routing, and enterprise solutions. It is transforming into AI infrastructure (photonics, data center connectivity).

Main Revenue Sources: Network infrastructure; AI/cloud customer revenue has grown rapidly in recent years (increasing its share in the most recent quarter).

Competitive Advantages: Accumulated 5G/optical network technology, global operator relationships, and the acquisition of Infinera enhancing photonics capabilities; outstanding cost-effectiveness in the AI ​​data center interconnect field.

Revenue: Approximately $20 billion TTM, with AI-related growth of 49% in some quarters.

Profit Margin: Improved gross margin (approximately 45.5% recently), net margin approximately 4%.

FCF/Liabilities: Stable cash flow and manageable debt.

Valuation: Trailing P/E ~85x (relatively high), Forward P/E ~36x, P/S ~3.3x. Reasonable compared to historical cycles, but with a significant premium from AI transformation; attractive growth compared to telecom peers.

Industry/Sector Position: Telecom infrastructure sector, currently showing divergent performance driven by AI. The company has a solid position in 5G/photonics, and the sector trend is shifting towards AI data center interconnection.

Recent News and Catalysts (last 1-4 weeks):

Positive: Trump mentions NOK, expanded AI cooperation with Amazon/Google, growth in AI infrastructure orders.

Negative: Cyclical fluctuations in telecom spending, overall market correction pressure.

Impact: Short-term (1-3 months) significant earnings catalyst; long-term (6-12 months) accelerated growth if AI transformation is implemented effectively.

Risks and Opportunities:

Risks: Telecom capital expenditure cycle, intensified competition, execution risks.

Opportunities: AI photonics orders, 5G/6G deployment, successful transformation.

Overall Assessment: Valuation is reasonably high (forward valuation is reasonable, trailing reflects transformation expectations). Bull Case: AI infrastructure orders exceeded expectations, and profit margins continue to expand. Bear Case: Telecom spending slowed, and transformation fell short of expectations.

2. $MRVL (Marvell Technology)

Company Overview: A semiconductor company focusing on data centers, custom AI chips, electro-optical interconnects, and enterprise networks.

Main Revenue Sources: Data centers (AI-driven, significantly increasing to 75%+), custom silicon, and electro-optical products.

Competitive Advantages: Deep collaboration with NVIDIA, custom AI ASIC design capabilities, and leading data center interconnect technology.

Financial Health (FY2026 Data):

Revenue: Record $8.195 billion in FY2026 (YoY +42%); strong growth in recent quarters such as Q2 and FY2027.

Profit Margin: Non-GAAP gross margin ~59%, significantly improved.

FCF: Strong operating cash flow.

Valuation: P/S/P/E elevated due to high growth (depending on the latest price, market cap is in the tens of billions of dollars). Significantly improved compared to historical levels, competitive with AI semiconductor peers.

Industry/Sector Position: Semiconductor/AI Chip Sector, currently fluctuating. The company's position in the data center AI chip/interconnect field is rising; the sector trend is driven by AI capex but is experiencing a short-term pullback.

Recent News and Catalysts:

Positive: Strong AI demand, NVIDIA collaboration, upward revision of revenue guidance.

Negative: Overall selling pressure in the sector, high valuation correction.

Impact: Short-term earnings/order catalysts; long-term growth in custom AI silicon.

Risks and Opportunities:

Risks: Slowing AI spending, competition, valuation compression.

Opportunities: Custom AI chip orders, data center expansion.

Overall Assessment: Valuation reflects high growth, reasonably high (AI premium). Bull Case: Continued outperformance in AI orders, revenue acceleration. Bear Case: Slower cycle or increased competition leading to lower-than-expected growth.

3. $MSFT (Microsoft Corporation)

Company Overview: Technology giant, with businesses covering cloud (Azure), software (Office), AI (Copilot/OpenAI), games, etc.

Key Revenue Streams: Intelligent Cloud (dominated by Azure), Productivity & Business Processes.

Competitive Advantages: Cloud market leadership, AI integration (Copilot), enterprise ecosystem lock-in, OpenAI collaboration.

Financial Health (FY2026 Q3 data):

Revenue: Q3 $82.9 billion (YoY +18%), Cloud $54.5 billion (+29%).

Profit Margin: Strong, with significant AI contribution.

FCF: Excellent, but CapEx is high (AI infrastructure).

Valuation: Reasonable Trailing/Forward P/E (growth stock premium), robust P/S. Attractive compared to historical levels and peers (technology cloud giants).

Industry/Sector Position: Cloud computing/AI software sector, currently performing steadily. The company is a cloud/AI leader, and the sector trend is shifting towards AI integration.

Recent News and Catalysts:

Positive Factors: High-speed growth in Cloud/AI revenue (AI annualized return of over 37 billion), Copilot adoption.

Negative Factors: High CapEx (AI investment), concerns about a slight slowdown in Azure growth.

Impact: Short-term earnings-driven; long-term AI monetization accelerated.

Risks and Opportunities:

Risks: Slow AI returns, CapEx pressure, competition.

Opportunities: Copilot monetization, expanding cloud market share, AI ecosystem.

Overall Assessment: Reasonable valuation (supported by growth). Bull Case: AI/Copilot exceeds expectations, Cloud accelerates. Bear Case: Delayed AI investment returns, slower growth.

Summary: All three companies deeply benefit from AI, but NOK is more geared towards telecom transformation, MRVL has high AI semiconductor purity, and MSFT is a leading player in both cloud and AI. Data timeliness is based on the latest financial reports; it is recommended to pay attention to their next financial reports. Market risks exist; the above is only an objective analysis.

Bright Solutions' COTS laser platforms; designed to support mission-critical applications:

https://t.co/Fx7Q4MKzyN]$

#LaserSystems #Photonics #LiDAR #DefenseTech #Engineering #LaserInnovation #BrightSolutions #RPMCLasers https://t.co/z6qWmvxw2n

🔍 New patent application #US20260172716A1 by #Samsung introduces a solid-state imaging device featuring a pixel array with single-photon avalanche diodes (SPADs) and a counter unit.
The counter unit includes a data generation circuit and memory, enhancing photon counting precision.
Explore innovations in #ImagingTech and #Photonics with this #PatentApplication.
$SSNLF

text:
I’ve already talked about Soitec $SOI  / $SLOIF in the past

However, the latest results have created a significant gap in the price, leaving the stock very attractive

Despite today’s rebound, it still looks very cheap, and a trend change has been confirmed

In my opinion, this is a good moment to enter $SOI / $SLOIF

Here are the results:

$SOI / $SLOIF reported weak results for its fiscal year 2026, with revenue of €592 million (-30% YoY) and EBITDA of €151 million (-49%)

These figures were mainly dragged down by weakness in its traditional segments, such as mobile and automotive markets

On the positive side, the company delivered solid 25% growth in its FD-SOI and Photonics-SOI lines

It particularly stood out in CPO substrates

Where $SOI / $SLOIF holds an almost complete monopoly and exceeded expectations by generating more than $100 million in revenue

In the long term, the investment thesis for $SOI / $SLOIF is based on the momentum that the Edge AI segment 

Through Photonics-SOI, could generate over the next 18-24 months

Driven by the ramp up of CPO technology and aligned with the timelines of key players like $TSMC and $NVDA

Very bullish on $SOI / $SLOIF

text:
Book Physics Course — Part 3: Electricity, Optics, and Atomic Structure Published in 1965 by A. V. Peryshkin and V. V. Krauklis

Physics Course — Part 3: Electricity, Optics, and Atomic Structure by A. V. Peryshkin and V. V. Krauklis is a seminal text in the history of Soviet secondary education. Its importance lies less in "secret" information and more in the pedagogical foundation it provided to generations of Soviet scientists and engineers who were responsible for the technological leaps of the mid-20th century.

The book serves as a rigorous bridge between classical Newtonian mechanics and the modern era of quantum physics. Below are the core takeaways organized by the text's primary sections.

1. Electricity and Electromagnetism

This section establishes the fundamental understanding of electromagnetic fields, currents, and circuit dynamics—principles that underpin heavy industrial technology and early computing infrastructure.

The Unified Field Perspective: The text emphasizes the inseparable nature of electric and magnetic phenomena, a precursor to mastering high-energy physics.

Electrodynamics as an Engineering Baseline: By grounding students in the behavior of electrons, currents, and fields, the curriculum prioritized the practical ability to design electrical systems, from basic circuitry to more complex high-power pulsed physics systems.

Methodological Rigor: The focus is on the direct relationship between force, matter, and motion, reinforcing a materialist approach to physics that treats electromagnetic fields as real, physical entities rather than abstract mathematical constructs.

2. Optics

The treatment of optics moves from classical geometric principles to the wave nature of light, which is essential for understanding the transition to advanced photonic and laser technologies.

Wave-Particle Duality: The text provides an early introduction to the concept that light behaves both as a wave (interference, diffraction) and as a particle (photons).  

Foundations for Photonics: Mastery of interference and diffraction patterns is the prerequisite for designing integrated circuits, fiber optics, and laser communication systems—fields where Soviet research achieved significant, often unacknowledged, early results.

3. Atomic Structure and Modern Physics

This section bridges the gap between macro-scale physics and the sub-atomic world, introducing the theoretical frameworks that allow for the manipulation of matter at the particle level.

The Atomic Model as a Tool: The text guides students through the evolution of atomic models, from early hypotheses to the quantum understanding of energy levels.

Conceptualizing "Modern" Phenomena: By introducing radioactivity, nuclear models, and the particle nature of matter, the text prepared students for the rapid developments in nuclear energy and materials science.

The Bridge to Quantum Mechanics: It explicitly introduces quantum concepts (wave-particle duality, energy quantization) not as speculative "fringe" theory, but as the fundamental reality governing the atom.

Join Dr. Ahu Gümrah Dumanli-Parry (🇬🇧University of Manchester) for a #seminar hosted by Dr. Emily Cranston on nature-inspired #photonic #materials. Learn how self-assembling cellulose-based systems can recreate optical architectures found in beetles and provide new routes to scalable, bio-inspired functional materials.

📍 CHBE 202, UBC Vancouver

text: I'll be honest. I think the AI models and the GPUs aren't efficient enough to generate the expected ROIs at this stage and it's going to lead to disappointments in the short-term. I think this will have index-level impacts. I'm out for a couple months, then heavy back into photonics/neuromorphic/ARM, and the usual solid moat companies.
We are investing in photonics - if you are a founder building the next Instagram, please reach out 🫡

text:
$AMD is getting close to becoming a trillion-dollar company

Mapping its supply chain seems like a smart choice

Some companies involved:

Packaging

PTI (https://t.co/hSYNb3Uyfl): AMD says it qualified the industry’s first 2.5D panel-based EFB interconnect with PTI, and PTI has reportedly landed AMD orders for panel-level packaging

ASE (https://t.co/TdQpyi8gtm / $ASX) is AMD’s partner for the next-generation 2.5D EFB bridge interconnect, and is also reportedly handling the packaging for AMD’s future MI500 CPO solution

Substrates

Unimicron (https://t.co/3zWLwnp3nz), Nan Ya PCB (https://t.co/GogrOx2kMX), and Kinsus (https://t.co/O71MimzVz2) are all AMD substrate suppliers, while Absolics / SKC (011790.KS) is close to AMD approval for glass substrates

Co-packaged optics (MI500)

GlobalFoundries ($GFS) is the reported fab partner for AMD’s future MI500 CPO

Sivers Semiconductors ($SIVE): Sivers’ laser arrays are being designed into GlobalFoundries’ silicon photonics reference designs, and GF is the company tied to AMD’s MI500 CPO, which make Sivers a potential supplier

Helios Rack

AIC (https://t.co/F7358yA5cU) reportedly handles the structural design for AMD’s Helios rack-scale platform

Celestica ($CLS) is the partner for Helios scale-up networking switches

Wiwynn (https://t.co/j9mRRWVdNF) is in AMD’s AI server ecosystem and has a strategic partnership with Ayar Labs to bring co-packaged optics into rack-scale systems

Wistron (https://t.co/hQ9zMr0LMt), Inventec (https://t.co/XI4RSDYZ4D), and Sanmina ($SANM) are partners for ODM, system integration, and manufacturing of AMD Helios

Portfolio update:

Today was one of those days where doing nothing was the trade.

No covered calls.
No CSPs.
No chasing.
No trimming.
Just let the AI infra book work.
Daily P&L: +2.74%
Open P&L: +87.26%
Cash: 21.17%
Current allocation:
$NBIS — 27.11%
Largest position, red today but still up big overall. Core neocloud position.
$MU — 21.71%
Monster day into earnings week. Anthropic partnership added even more confirmation to the memory thesis.

Cash — 21.17%
Still keeping dry powder. Cash is a weapon, not a drag.
$MRVL — 8.07%
Laggard today, but still a core AI interconnect/custom silicon position.
$CRDO — 6.79%
Huge day. Connectivity trade is alive and well.

$DRAM — 6.27%
Memory basket continues to work with $MU.

$VRT — 4.28%
Power/cooling still confirming the AI data center buildout.
$COHR — 3.36%
Photonics/laser chokepoint had a monster move.

$ALAB — 1.31%
Still too small. I want more over time, but not chasing vertical candles.
Big lesson today:

The clean book won.
No options capping the winners.
No obligations forcing decisions.
No overtrading.

Sometimes the most professional move is sitting on your hands while the thesis plays out.

text:
Here $SIVE explains that autonomous systems (self-driving vehicles, robots or EdgeAI) need two essential things to work well

One of its many areas of expertise, as we’ve already mentioned isn’t only the photonics

Precise detection:

Thanks to its FMCW LiDAR technology, which uses high-power DFB lasers

This solution measures both distance and object speed with great accuracy

Even when they are moving, while keeping energy consumption low

Reliable connectivity:

Its high frequency wireless solutions (mmWave) enable fast

Low latency communication between devices essential for making real time decisions

$SIVE offers both the “eyes” (advanced sensing) and the “voice” (robust connectivity) that make safe and efficient autonomy possible in the future
embedded_text:

text:
#truefreefinance  (June 27.2026 )Robotics is quietly emerging as the next major wave in tech investing
Recent data from Andreessen Horowitz and PitchBook (March update) suggests that investment activity in robotics is accelerating, with both funding and deal flow continuing to rise.
What makes this trend particularly interesting is its connection to the broader AI infrastructure cycle.
Many of the same sectors benefiting from AI data center expansion are also positioned to play a role in robotics and humanoid systems:
• Memory (DRAM & NAND) → supporting on-device AI inference and storage
• Photonics & optics → enabling machine vision, LiDAR, and sensing systems
• AI compute infrastructure → powering autonomous decision-making in real-world environments
At the moment, most exposure is still indirect — through upstream suppliers or large tech companies investing heavily in robotics, such as Amazon and Tesla.
But the bigger question for investors is forward-looking:
If capital markets reopen strongly in 2026–2027, will we start seeing more pure-play robotics and humanoid companies coming to public markets?
If that happens, robotics could move from a niche theme to a full investment cycle of its own.
Not financial advice. Based on publicly available market data and industry trends.
#AI #Robotics #HumanoidRobots #Technology #Investing #FutureTech #StockMarket

text:
Nvidia quietly became the #1 data center Ethernet switch vendor — $2.1B revenue, 21.5% market share, up from 4% two years ago. IDC calls it 'one of the most significant vendor landscape shifts' in enterprise networking history. The networking business tripled to $15B.

The obvious read: Nvidia is diversifying beyond GPUs.

The more strategic read: Nvidia is identifying the next AI bottleneck before it fully materializes, then buying or building the solution before anyone else realizes it's a problem.

At million-GPU cluster scale, the binding constraint isn't compute or even memory anymore. It's the fabric connecting them. Copper interconnects hit hard walls on power, bandwidth density, and reach. GPU utilization tanks when all-reduce and collective communication across racks can't keep up.

Every cluster operator has experienced this: plenty of FLOPs, terrible effective throughput because the network is the bottleneck.

Nvidia saw this coming when it bought Mellanox for $6.9B in 2019. Spectrum-X now generates more revenue than all Ethernet networking peers combined, per Jensen. The company is vertically integrating end-to-end cluster fabric the same way it integrated CUDA into the software stack.

The next phase: photonics. Nvidia's CPO roadmap with TSMC COUPE is preparing for optical interconnects at the rack level. Bandwidth per AI rack goes from ~130 TB/s to multiples beyond that.

Here's the under-covered part: the photonics supply chain — lasers, silicon photonics foundries, fiber attach, packaging — is even earlier in its high-volume ramp than HBM was in 2024. The qualification cycles are longer, yields lower, and the ecosystem less mature.

The AI bottleneck narrative has shifted: compute → memory → networking → optics.

$15B in networking revenue is the market signaling the next constraint. The companies that solve the photonics supply chain will be the Micron and SK Hynix of the next cycle.

#Nvidia #Photonics #AIChips #Networking

IDC calls it 'one of the most significant vendor landscape shifts' in enterprise networking history. The networking business tripled to $15B. The obvious read: Nvidia is diversifying beyond GPUs. The more strategic read: Nvidia is identifying the next AI bottleneck before it fully materializes, then buying or building the solution before anyone else realizes it's a problem. At million-GPU cluster scale, the binding constraint isn't compute or even memory anymore. It's the fabric connecting them. Copper interconnects hit hard walls on power, bandwidth density, and reach. GPU utilization tanks when all-reduce and collective communication across racks can't keep up. Every cluster operator has experienced this: plenty of FLOPs, terrible effective throughput because the network is the bottleneck. Nvidia saw this coming when it bought Mellanox for $6.9B in 2019. Spectrum-X now generates more revenue than all Ethernet networking peers combined, per Jensen. The company is vertically integrating end-to-end cluster fabric the same way it integrated CUDA into the software stack. The next phase: photonics. Nvidia's CPO roadmap with TSMC COUPE is preparing for optical interconnects at the rack level. Bandwidth per AI rack goes from ~130 TB/s to multiples beyond that. Here's the under-covered part: the photonics supply chain — lasers, silicon photonics foundries, fiber attach, packaging — is even earlier in its high-volume ramp than HBM was in 2024. The qualification cycles are longer, yields lower, and the ecosystem less mature. The AI bottleneck narrative has shifted: compute → memory → networking → optics. $15B in networking revenue is the market signaling the next constraint. text:
Retail is waiting for pure-play robotics IPOs, while institutional money is quietly locking up the raw physical supply chains today.

The vertical spike highlighted confirms a massive paradigm shift: capital is moving aggressively into physical, embodied AI. As the first pure-play humanoid exits open the liquidity floodgates for H2 into 2027, the structural allocation war we are seeing in data centers will expand exponentially into robotics.

The smart money is front-running this cross-exposure overlap through critical upstream hardware:
 The Memory Squeeze Phase II: A humanoid robot running dense, real-time localized inference models requires up to a 10x memory multiplier over regular edge hardware. The exact same HBM, DRAM, and NAND components currently supply-constrained through 2027 to feed hyperscalers are facing an entirely new, massive downstream demand sink.
 The Photonics Vision Choke Point: You cannot run autonomous, real-time spatial manipulation on a gutted hardware spec sheet. Next-gen FMCW LiDAR vision and sensor frameworks rely directly on high-power DFB laser chips. Companies like $AAOI or $LITE that control internal MOCVD crystal growth foundries aren't just building for transceivers—they are the literal gatekeepers of the physical optical sensing layer that every single humanoid platform requires to operate safely.

The Upstream Component Leverage: Retail is waiting for pure-play robotics IPOs to buy the hype, completely missing that the absolute pricing power sits with the upstream component layer right now. Whether the end-program is run by $TSLA , $AMZN or a venture-backed pure-play, they all must secure raw, physical capacity from the exact same tightly contracted component suppliers.

The cognition bottleneck has officially shifted to a physical execution bottleneck. When the factories are fully booked through mid-2027 just to satisfy data center demand, adding a multi-billion dollar humanoid hardware ramp into the equation means the choke points capture all the structural alpha.

Accumulating the physical plumbing before the global IPO season reprices the entire supply chain. Long the bottlenecks. 📈🤖🧠

text:
🧵 My personal market heuristic after tracking AI infrastructure themes closely:

Markets tend to price things in ~8 months in advance — except Space, Robotics, and Quantum, where sentiment runs well ahead of fundamentals.

Here's how I'm applying it across themes I follow: 👇

Photonics has a clear roadmap:

• 1.6T pluggables ( $LITE, $COHR, $AAOI) are hitting now
• CPO Scale-Out ramp: late H2 2026
• CPO Scale-Up (Ayar, Celestial, etc.): H2 2027

By the 8M rule, CPO Scale-Up names are still ~4 months early to position heavily.

Memory is mid-bottleneck and re-rating accordingly:

• HBM3e: now ($MU, Samsung, SK Hynix)
• HBM4 mass production: ramping now
• HBF: 2027

Same players across cycles = compounding opportunity as each architecture matures.

Glass Substrates:

• SKC Absolics early ramp: H2 2026
• Volume inflection: H2 2027–2028
• TSMC CoPoS, Samsung, others: 2027–2028

Machine/equipment suppliers here tend to see revenue before the volume ramp — worth noting for positioning.

Neoclouds: Q4 2026 earnings looks like a key inflection window for leading players.

In hindsight, March 2026 was the entry, not September 2025. The ~8M rule would've caught that.

Current repositioning focus:
• Memory
• Neoclouds
• Photonics
• Robotics

Preference for names capturing multiple themes simultaneously (e.g., pluggable + NPO + CPO exposure) and tactical exposure to bottleneck plays like advanced packaging and MLCCs.

Key personal lesson: Avoid heavy exposure to names 12M+ out. Early conviction is fine — overweighting too early is where sizing discipline matters most.

Still learning. These are just my own frameworks — not advice. 🙏

text:
Reflections in the Dip: Why My Conviction in $SIVE Has Never Been Stronger

I’ve been watching the recent pullback with a strange sense of calm.

Weak hands are panicking. That’s what they do when the price moves against them for a few weeks. But if you’ve been here before, you know this feeling isn’t new, it’s just the market doing what it always does to test conviction.

Over two years ago we were trading around 1.8 SEK. That drop felt brutal at the time. This one? This is noise compared to that. And yet the company is in a completely different place today.

We’re still in an industry that hasn’t really started yet. Photonics for AI data centers is still pre-ramp for most people. There are no shiny consumer products on shelves for the average investor to point at and say “that’s what I own.” No flashy earnings beats every quarter. Just deep technology, long qualification cycles, and a market that very few truly understand. Most people want to see something they can touch before they believe. That’s why these moves feel so violent, because the narrative isn’t obvious until it suddenly is.

That’s also why wireless has been so important. It’s been the quiet engine running in the background, delivering real revenue and building the foundation while photonics gets ready for its moment. Most of the market still doesn’t price that in properly. They’re too focused on the “when does photonics happen?” question and miss that the company is already executing on multiple fronts.

I expect photonics news and orders from Ayar Labs, $POET, O-Net, $AEVA and $JBL before the year is out. Those won’t just be headlines, they’ll be real validation that starts to de-risk the story and reduce some of this volatility. I’m 100% convinced of that. Not because I’m hoping. Because I’ve followed this company long enough to know how it works.

Nobody believes until it’s too late.

We saw it from 4 SEK to over 100 SEK. Almost nobody saw that coming when it was actually happening. The next leg won’t be any different.

There’s a lot of games being played right now, short sellers, institutions, traders all trying to take their piece. That’s normal when a stock has moved this much. It will stop when they’re done. Right now we’re also at a price level where a lot of serious money starts paying attention again. Don’t let the noise fool you.

Do your own work.

Read the presentations. Watch the CMDs. Look at the customer pipeline, the partnerships, the technology edge. Build your own picture instead of scrolling through what everyone else is saying.

If you don’t believe light is the future of high-speed connectivity, that’s fine. There are plenty of other sectors. But if you do understand the technology and what Sivers actually brings to the table, the differentiated InP platform, the asset-light model, the real design wins, then the choice becomes pretty obvious.

This is still a market that very few people truly grasp. A market that doesn’t even fully know how big it can get. And if you play it right, it rewards patience like few others.
I actually believe more in $SIVEF today than I did a couple of years ago.

Why? Because I can see the moves that are coming. Dual listing preparations are real. They’re not walking onto Nasdaq empty-handed. Watch what happens over the next few months. A lot of people are going to wish they had listened earlier.

For what it’s worth, I haven’t sold a single share. And I won’t. The day I sell is the day the company stops delivering. Not when the stock has a bad week. Not when the noise gets loud. Price and company are two different things.

This is still the early innings.

Stay calm. Do the work. Trust what you know.

text:
ちょうど昨日Zackの記事もありました、GFSとUMCの投資ロジックの違いについて

1. 成長ドライバー：シリコンフォトニクス vs. 22nm成熟プロセス
 GlobalFoundries (GFS) —— 高成長なニッチ市場への注力：
GFSはターゲットを絞ったアプローチをとっており、AIやデータセンターのインフラ需要を直接取り込んでいます。特に、光通信向けのシリコンフォトニクス (Silicon Photonics) 技術が牽引しており、2026年には同事業の売上が倍増、2028年末にはランレートで10億ドルを突破する見込みです。また、高周波通信向けのシリコンゲルマニウム (SiGe) ターンキーソリューションは2027年までキャパシティが埋まっています。AI光通信チェーンや欧米のローカルサプライチェーンのテーマに投資したい場合、GFSのストーリーは非常に魅力的です。
 UMC (聯電) —— 規模の経済とコスト効率：
UMCは成熟プロセスの雄であり、主力である22nmプラットフォームが業績を支えています。2026年末までに50社以上の顧客がテープアウト（設計完了）を予定しており、ディスプレイ駆動ICやマイクロコントローラ（MCU）など、幅広い汎用チップを網羅しています。GFSのような派手なAIインフラのテーマ性は薄いものの、Intelと共同開発している12nmプロセスが2027年に量産開始予定であり、将来的に高付加価値市場への参入ロードマップを描いています。

2. 財務パフォーマンスとバリュエーション
直近の2026年第1四半期（Q1）決算では、両社ともに売上総利益率（グロス・マージン）を約29%に維持していますが、その中身は異なります。
 GFS（バリュエーションは高め、長期的価格支配力）： スマートフォン市場の減速によりモバイル部門が逆風を受けているものの、デザインウィン（設計獲得）が前年同期比で50%急増しています。これは顧客の囲い込みが順調である証拠であり、長期的な価格支配力の強さを示しています。
 UMC（稼働率回復による短期的な利益爆発力）： Q1の設備稼働率が79%（前年同期から10ポイント上昇）まで回復したことで、営業レバレッジが効き、純利益が大幅に急増しました。また、UMCは伝統的に配当が安定しており配当利回りが高いため、インカムゲインを重視する投資家にとっても安心感があります。

3. どちらが今日の優れた投資機会か？
投資スタイルやポートフォリオの戦略によって結論が分かれます。
📥 GlobalFoundries (GFS) を選ぶべきケース：
長期的なアルファ（市場平均を超える超過リターン）を狙う場合です。
主力であるモバイル向けビジネスが2026年後半まで高一桁の減少を見込むなど短期的には逆風がありますが、米国・ドイツ・シンガポールに分散された製造拠点は、地政学的リスクを嫌う大手クライアント（Appleやルネサスなど）のニーズに合致しています。プレミアムなマルチプル（株価倍率）を許容し、AIインフラや光ネットワークへの構造転換に賭けたい投資家向けです。
📥 UMC (聯電) を選ぶべきケース：
景気サイクル好転による安心感と、確実なキャッシュフローを狙う場合です。
現在のUMCの主な懸念点は、シンガポール工場の拡張に伴う巨額の減価償却費であり、これが2026年後半の利益率を圧迫するリスクがあります。しかし、世界的な自動車、産業、家電向けチップの需要が本格的に回復すると予想する場合、稼働率上昇がそのまま利益に直結する高い財務レバレッジと、下値リスクを抑える高配当（防衛力）を兼ね備えた、バランスの良い循環株として非常に優秀です。

text:
Q1 2026 just printed a record: $16.3B invested across ~492 deals in robotics & physical AI. Humanoids are eating a massive slice of that capital. 

Deal sizes in the hundreds of millions are now normal. The steep upward trend you highlighted is accelerating into deployment phase, not just demos.
The cross-exposure angle is one of the highest-conviction parts of this story:

Memory (DRAM/HBM + NAND): Humanoids need serious onboard inference + storage. Same AI data center stack that’s already in hyper-growth directly feeds edge humanoid brains.

Photonics & sensing (DFB lasers for FMCW LiDAR): This is the vision/perception layer that turns clunky robots into dexterous, safe collaborators. Overlap with advanced auto/robotics supply chains is real and underappreciated.

Right now most investable exposure is still upstream components or inside large-cap winners like $TSLA and $AMZN (warehouse deployments scaling today).
But the pure-play IPO/exit wave is no longer theoretical:

Tesla is converting Fremont Model S/X lines — Optimus V3 production ramp starts Summer 2026. Public sales targeted toward late 2027. This is the strongest signal yet that 2026 is the bridge year from prototype to early volume.

Unitree (China’s volume leader — already shipping thousands of humanoids/quadrupeds with ~60% gross margins) filed its STAR Market IPO in March targeting ~$7B valuation. First major liquidity event.

Agility Robotics (Digit bots already working with Amazon, GXO, etc.) is going public via SPAC soon (expected ticker $AGLT).

Global IPO season for pure-play humanoids & robotics platforms is kicking off in H2 2026 and runs hot into 2027

text:
@OpenAI’s first custom chip, Jalapeño, built with @Broadcom for inference.

Everyone’s reading it as a shot at @nvidia. 

The deeper signal is that at this scale, the bottleneck isn’t just the compute, it’s the data moving between them.

Copper’s running out and Broadcom’s other big bet is the answer is photonics.

#photonics #copper #ai #openai

text:
Weekly portfolio update:

A rough week for the portfolio finishing
9.8% down 🔴

My photonics names $SIVE & $IQE took big hits this week: $SIVE down 29% & $IQE 7%.

Rolls Royce had a pretty flat week, down 0.14%🔴

All holdings in my pie finished red for the week too.

$VWRP -1.63%🔴
$SEMI -5.31%🔴
$QNTG -3.65%🔴

I crossed £2,000 last Friday, I’m now back under despite depositing £181 this week.

How was your week?👇

text:
Sivers Semi ($SIVE) is presenting a strong buying opportunity on this pullback.

The core fundamentals remain fully intact. The company is well-positioned in AI data center optics and photonics with a solid pipeline, growing demand for their laser and interconnect tech, and real execution potential ahead.

The recent downside appears driven largely by short seller pressure and manipulation — high short interest that’s disconnected from the underlying business strength.

This is a dip worth considering for those focused on AI infrastructure. Long-term setup looks compelling.

Anyone else buying today?

text:
The disconnect here is purely structural: local European markets often fail to price deep-tech optionality correctly, misinterpreting strategic capital expansion as structural weakness.

For $SIVE, migrating to NASDAQ isn't just about liquidity; it’s about shifting the valuation to institutional investors who actually understand upstream photonics architecture and 60% GM profiles.

text:
Bears most of the time actually don't misunderstand. 

They reframe it to fill their egos on why they are on calling out stocks like $SIVE and $AAOI not being good buys today.

It is so funny how most of the market goes against these names when things are down due to macro conditions, with nothing related actually to the fundamentals/thesis of such companies. 

InP laser shortage is going to kick in next year. 1.6T/CPO timelines will keep compressing.

Obviously the gross margins and revenue growth will show on the balance sheet of these photonic companies. Afterall, it is the next bull cycle of the overall AI buildout.

Everyone betted against $MU, $SNDK last year. Now they are the so called bulls that called it early. 2026 is showing memory shortage.

A lot of investments & money is going into delivering when ramps start to scale in 2027. This was confirmed by earnings from $LITE, $COHR, $AAOI, and many others. 

BoFA and GS reports back this well. They aren't your cheap X account researches that throw out rubbish. These are institutions with a big ecosystem connection to the overall technological industry, including AI companies. Analysts, experts and technicians in the field write these reports that take weeks (maybe months), not just a few days of research. 

After all being said and research by @aleabitoreddit and all others, if one still doesn't believe in the story playing out and why this is true, I think it would be good to reconsider and have a check in the mirror if you are just a soft hand in the market with absolutely zero real conviction wherever you invest. Today it could be photonics. Tomorrow you'll find another one. 

Some habits don't change. It requires a change in the overall mindset and character of the investor. That is why I always reinforce the need to construct your own system, and not blindly follow anybody. Even me. 

- AI

text:
Who is Coherent ( $COHR )? 

Coherent is a leader in photonics and laser technology, supplying high-speed optical components used in AI data centers and advanced networking.

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang recently broke ground on Coherent’s new U.S. laser factory, showing that NVIDIA is securing optics supply early the same strategy it used with HBM and advanced packaging.

Financials & Outlook
• Datacom segment showing solid growth driven by AI demand
• Focus on margin expansion while building next-gen photonics capacity
• AI optics viewed as a key multi-year growth driver into 2026–2027

What Separates Coherent
• Broader portfolio than pure transceiver plays like $AAOI 
• Deeper vertical integration (laser chips to modules) vs. $LITE
• Direct $NVDA partnership on domestic manufacturing adds strategic weight

The recent pullback has created a small entry point on a name with growing importance in the AI supply chain. 

Let me know what you guys think about this one has it ran its course or is it still a buy ?

text:
Millimeter-wave (mmWave) technology is powering the next generation of ultra-fast wireless communication. Discover innovative antennas, electro-optic modulators, and key components driving 5G and beyond. 

https://t.co/TIc1uCneZN

#5G #mmWave #Photonics #Wireless #Telecom https://t.co/pZV8Uzeg9y

$IQE
One of the most important points from FY2025, in my view, was that IQE is seeing increasing customer demand for 6/8-inch GaSb-based epiwafers, while its LWIR product has achieved production qualification with a Tier 1 customer.

I believe infrared-based LWIR/MWIR, and potentially SWIR as well, could become a very important growth pillar for IQE through 2030.

IQE’s sensing portfolio can broadly be divided into four platforms:

GaAs: VCSEL / 3D sensing
InP: SWIR / photonics
GaN: LiDAR drivers / microLED
GaSb: IR detectors / T2SL / MWIR / LWIR

In other words, IQE is a rare pure-play merchant epitaxy foundry that owns all the key III-V platforms: GaAs, InP, GaN, and GaSb.

Among these, the area I am particularly focused on is GaSb-based MWIR/LWIR.

Robotics, drones, autonomous driving, and defence systems are still in the early stages of growth.

Today, many systems combine RGB cameras, LiDAR, radar, ToF, and other sensors, but as these systems become more advanced, sensing will not end with RGB cameras alone.

RGB cameras are extremely powerful in the visible spectrum.

They are excellent for recognizing colour, shape, text, and surface-level information.

However, visible-light-based data has clear limitations.

At night, in smoke, dust, fog, strong backlight, camouflage, and adverse weather, RGB cameras alone cannot provide sufficiently robust information.

Future robots, drones, and unmanned systems will not simply need to “see” what is visible.

They will also need to understand the thermal information emitted by people, vehicles, engines, batteries, and industrial equipment.

This is where MWIR/LWIR becomes increasingly important.

MWIR is linked to high-temperature objects, engines, missiles, long-range surveillance, aerospace, and defence systems.

LWIR is linked to people, animals, machine heat, night surveillance, thermal imaging, and hazardous-environment perception.

In other words, MWIR/LWIR may not merely be a “supporting sensor” for future autonomous systems.

In certain environments, it could become a core perception layer that is even more important than RGB.

This is especially true in defence.

The future battlefield is moving toward drones, unmanned vehicles, surveillance systems, automatic target recognition, and night operations.

In these environments, high-resolution RGB images alone are not enough.

To detect people in darkness, vehicles with residual engine heat, hidden equipment, missile-launch heat signatures, or abnormal machine heat, MWIR/LWIR thermal sensing is required.

The same logic applies to robotics.

If humanoids or industrial robots only operate in bright indoor environments, RGB + depth sensors may be enough.

But once they expand into dark factories, warehouses, night patrol, disaster sites, military robotics, and machine overheating detection, the story changes.

For robots, MWIR/LWIR can become both “eyes that see at night” and a “sense that detects heat.”

The problem is that existing MWIR/LWIR sensors have been very expensive.

High-performance IR detectors have been costly because of small substrates, complex epitaxy, low yields, ROIC hybrid bonding, cryocoolers, and expensive optics.

As a result, they have mainly been used in markets that can absorb the cost, such as defence, aerospace, and specialised industrial applications.

But IQE’s 6/8-inch GaSb-based epiwafer directly targets one of these bottlenecks.

When wafer size increases, it is not simply about making the wafer bigger.

The number of dies per wafer increases, large-area manufacturing productivity improves, and there is room for better uniformity and yield.

This creates the foundation for producing high resolution FPAs focal plane arrays more economically.

Simply put, high performance thermal sensing, which was previously too expensive and limited mainly to defence and specialised applications, may have the potential to move into broader markets through large diameter epi platforms.

IQE is not talking about this as a simple R&D project.

It has mentioned both Tier 1 production qualification for its LWIR product and increasing demand for 6/8-inch GaSb-based epiwafers.

As humanoid physical AI models mature and need more sensing vectors to become robust against real-world noise, I believe there will come a point where additional modalities become necessary.

In autonomous driving, this could happen even earlier, with MWIR/LWIR potentially being integrated sooner.

The fact that production qualification has already been completed with a Tier 1 customer suggests that this demand is not theoretical anymore. It may already be starting.

Beyond data centres, how do you view IQE’s future in robotics and autonomous driving?

text:
خلال السنوات القليلة الماضية شهدت الأسواق انتقالات متسارعة لرأس المال بين موجات تكنولوجية متعاقبة.

بدأت الموجة مع السيارات الكهربائية والطاقة النظيفة ثم انتقل التركيز إلى الذكاء الاصطناعي تلاه الاهتمام بالحوسبة الكمية ثم البنية التحتية لمراكز البيانات والطاقة اللازمة لدعم الاقتصاد الرقمي الجديد.

لكن تاريخ الابتكار يشير إلى أن كل ثورة تقنية تخلق احتياجات جديدة وتمهد الطريق للثورة التالية.

وفي تقديري فإن المرحلة القادمة قد تتمحور حول التقنيات التي تدمج الذكاء الاصطناعي بالعالم المادي والإنسان نفسه.

الروبوتات البشرية وواجهات الدماغ والحاسوب والحوسبة الضوئية والتكنولوجيا الحيوية المدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي قد تمثل الركائز الأساسية للعقد القادم.

السؤال الذي يجب أن يطرحه المستثمرون ليس ما هو القطاع الأكثر زخماً اليوم؟
بل ما هي التقنيات التي ستعيد تشكيل الاقتصاد العالمي خلال السنوات العشر القادمة؟

فالأسواق لا تكافئ الإجماع.

إنها تكافئ الرؤية المبكرة.

text:
Die Verlagerung der Halbleitersicherungsqualität von Siliziumkarbid hin zur optischen KI Infrastruktur

Der explosive Anstieg der Rechenlasten in modernen Rechenzentren verändert die Anforderungen an die physische Zuverlässigkeit von Mikrochips grundlegend. Da der Ausfall einzelner Komponenten in massiven KI Clustern immense finanzielle Schäden verursacht, rücken spezialisierte Verfahren zur Qualitätsprüfung direkt auf Wafer-Ebene ins Zentrum der globalen Wertschöpfungskette.
Das US Halbleiterprüfunternehmen Aehr Test Systems demonstriert diese transformationale Dynamik in der ersten Hälfte des Jahres 2026 durch einen beispiellosen Kursanstieg an den Finanzmärkten. Die Aktie, die zu Beginn des Jahres noch nahe der 20 US Dollar Marke notierte, kletterte im Juni 2026 zeitweise auf über 115 US Dollar. Dieser Zuwachs spiegelt den erfolgreichen strategischen Wandel des Unternehmens wider. Während Aehr Test Systems in den vergangenen Jahren primär von Systemen für Siliziumkarbid-Leistungshalbleiter im stagnierenden Elektromobilitätssektor abhing, positioniert sich der Konzern nun als kritischer Infrastrukturpartner für die optische Datenübertragung in KI Rechenzentren.
Den technologischen Hebel bildet die proprietäre FOX-XP Plattform, ein vollautomatisiertes System für das sogenannte Wafer-Level Burn-In. Bei diesem Verfahren werden hunderte Halbleiter-Wafer gleichzeitig unter extremen thermischen und elektrischen Bedingungen künstlich gealtert, um fehlerhafte Bauteile noch vor dem finalen Verpackungsprozess zu identifizieren. Ein entscheidender Engpass moderner Beschleuniger-Cluster liegt in der Kommunikation über optische Transceiver (Silicon Photonics), die Lichtsignale statt elektrischer Impulse nutzen. Da diese optischen Schnittstellen extrem anfällig für frühe Ausfälle sind, verlangen führende Hyperscaler eine lückenlose Vorabprüfung.
Die finanzielle Bestätigung dieser Neuausrichtung lieferten die jüngsten Auftragseingänge. Nach einem vorangegangenen, operativ schwächeren Quartal mit lediglich 10,3 Millionen US Dollar Umsatz meldete Aehr für die zweite Jahreshälfte 2026 einen massiven Sprung bei den Buchungen auf über 92 Millionen US Dollar. Treiber war eine Serie von Großaufträgen, darunter eine Rekordbestellung über 41 Millionen US Dollar von einem führenden Hyperscale-KI Kunden sowie Folgeaufträge eines globalen Marktführers für Netzwerklösungen. Diese Systeme dienen sowohl der technologischen Qualifikation neuer Chip-Designs als auch der anschließenden Hochvolumenproduktion.
Trotz dieser operativen Meilensteine bleibt die Aktie an den Kapitalmärkten von hoher Volatilität geprägt, was sich in einer temporären Korrektur Ende Juni auf rund 91 US Dollar äußerte. Mit einem Kurs-Umsatz-Verhältnis von über 60 reflektiert die Bewertung bereits erhebliche Vorschusslorbeeren auf die kommenden Fiskaljahre. Da das Unternehmen den kapitalintensiven Hochlauf zudem über ein aktives Aktienplatzierungsprogramm (At-The-Market) mitfinanziert, müssen die anstehenden Quartalszahlen eine fehlerfreie Skalierung der Auslieferungen belegen. Für den Technologiesektor untermauert der Fall Aehr jedoch ein fundamentales Prinzip: Der wirtschaftliche Erfolg im KI Zyklus verschiebt sich zunehmend von den reinen Chip-Designern hin zu den hochspezialisierten Enablern, welche die physische Betriebssicherheit des Gesamtsystems garantieren.

text:
Personally looking at Quantum Technology "RIGID" stocks

here's what I think overall

May 21st, $2 Billion+ was committed to the CHIPS and Science Act, plus two executive orders on June 22nd to reinforce this:
> quantum computing got DoE 
> migrate to post-quantum computing by 2030 by agencies

There are 9 companies in US stocks that this can affect directly

The RIGID stocks imo have the higher importance
Rigetti $RGTI 
IMB $IBM 
Global Foundations $GFS 
Infleqion $INFQ 
D-Wave $QBTS 

With these, the two that I have personal interest are IMB and GFS. 

IMB needs to introduction here and technically the money goes to Anderson, but its still the front runner of superconducting for 2028 and having domestic but indirect infrastructure is still a benefit to the company. 

GFS is focused on all quantum based architecture, but committed to AI Data Centers domestically, called SCALE for high bandwidth but energy efficient connectivity 

I have been looking for a company that will release the pressure valve on demand and GFS could do it in the same way that broadband helped de-pressurize the telecom days.

I am not as bullish on QBTS for example, I think they have too much rising debts and revenue fell 81%, but could get a partnership or acquisition. 

RGTI def worth a watch with their $NVDA NVQLink partnership and Cepheus launch 

The other 4 stocks worth mentioning:
Atom Computing 
DIraq
PsiQuantum 
Quantinnum 

These are mainly supply chain stocks for sensors and photonics but could be part of the discussion. They could certainly do well or bad, but I think the RIGIDs would help them rise, so better imo to focus on the main stack. 

Ive heard about quantum technology for a long time

Is it finally here?

Technology "RIGID" stocks here's what I think overall May 21st, $2 Billion+ was committed to the CHIPS and Science Act, plus two executive orders on June 22nd to reinforce this: > quantum computing got DoE > migrate to post-quantum computing by 2030 by agencies There are 9 companies in US stocks that this can affect directly The RIGID stocks imo have the higher importance Rigetti $RGTI IMB $IBM Global Foundations $GFS Infleqion $INFQ D-Wave $QBTS With these, the two that I have personal interest are IMB and GFS. IMB needs to introduction here and technically the money goes to Anderson, but its still the front runner of superconducting for 2028 and having domestic but indirect infrastructure is still a benefit to the company. GFS is focused on all quantum based architecture, but committed to AI Data Centers domestically, called SCALE for high bandwidth but energy efficient connectivity I have been looking for a company that will release the pressure valve on demand and GFS could do it in the same way that broadband helped de-pressurize the telecom days. I am not as bullish on QBTS for example, I think they have too much rising debts and revenue fell 81%, but could get a partnership or acquisition. RGTI def worth a watch with their $NVDA NVQLink partnership and Cepheus launch The other 4 stocks worth mentioning: Atom Computing DIraq PsiQuantum Quantinnum These are mainly supply chain stocks for sensors and photonics but could be part of the discussion. They could certainly do well or bad, but I think the RIGIDs would help them rise, so better imo to focus on the main stack. Ive heard about quantum technology for a long time Is it finally here?"}}}, "rest_id": "2069839117286879711", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "676", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Ms. Tewari brings over 35 years of experience in corporate operations and manufacturing leadership—spanning the aerospace, defense, telecommunications, and advanced industrial sectors—with a focus on laser technology.

She has held senior leadership roles overseeing multi-site operations across North America and Europe, managing complex and highly regulated manufacturing activities at organizations such as Northrop Grumman, Raytheon, Comtech Telecommunications, and Pratt & Whitney.

In her role at Laser Photonics, Ms. Tewari will oversee global operations, leading manufacturing optimization and the scaling of enterprise processes to enhance productivity, quality, and delivery performance in support of sustainable growth.

"Ann joins us at an exciting time for the company," said Wayne Tupuola, CEO of Laser Photonics. "Following two major acquisitions that expanded our operations, we are now focused on refining our processes and aligning strategies across the organization."

text:
Me canso, no te lo repito más 🎯

SOITEC $SOI.PA controla prácticamente el cuello de botella de una tecnología clave para los smartphones, los autos eléctricos y ahora… la inteligencia artificial ‼️
Es el líder mundial indiscutido en obleas de Silicon-on-Insulator (SOI). Su tecnología patentada Smart Cut, desarrollada junto con el CEA-Leti, es una especie de ‘escalpelo atómico’: permite transferir una capa ultrafina de silicio cristalino perfecto sobre un aislante. Eso reduce fugas de energía, mejora el rendimiento y baja el consumo.
Gracias a esto, Soitec domina el mercado de sustratos avanzados. 

‼️En Photonics-SOI —el material base para los transceptores ópticos que están revolucionando los data centers de IA— está cerca del monopolio. Prácticamente todas las grandes fundiciones que fabrican chips fotónicos para IA dependen de sus obleas.
En el móvil, sus obleas RF-SOI están dentro de casi todos los smartphones premium del mundo, mejorando la señal 5G y el consumo de batería. En automoción, sus soluciones Power-SOI y FD-SOI ayudan a que los vehículos eléctricos y los sistemas ADAS sean más eficientes.
La compañía facturó cerca de 600 millones de euros en su último ejercicio fiscal, tuvo un año complicado por corrección de inventarios en móviles, pero su negocio de Photonics-SOI para IA ya superó los 100 millones de dólares y sigue creciendo fuerte.
Con más de 2.100 empleados, plantas en Francia y Singapur, y más de 4.300 patentes, Soitec es uno de esos jugadores invisibles pero críticos de la cadena de suministro de semiconductores. Sin ellos, muchos de los avances en eficiencia energética y en la explosión de la IA simplemente serían mucho más caros o difíciles de escalar.
Una joya tecnológica francesa que vale la pena seguir de cerca. 🇫🇷🔬

text:
🚨 Jensen Huang in SHERMAN, TX?!? 🚨

For those of you asking, "Who in the world is Jensen Huang?" ... keep reading.

What was once a quiet North Texas town is quickly becoming a major semiconductor and technology hub. We're not talking about AI chatbots writing emails or generating funny pictures. We're talking about the technology that makes AI possible in the first place.

That's a big reason why Jensen Huang visited Sherman. As AI demand explodes, the need for high-speed optical networking and photonics is growing right alongside it.

If you've never heard of him, he's the CEO of NVIDIA. The company at the center of the AI revolution and one of the most valuable companies on the planet.

NVIDIA has committed to producing up to $500 billion in AI infrastructure in the United States, including significant investments right here in Texas.

Last week, Coherent broke ground on an expanded Sherman facility that will help power the next generation of AI infrastructure. The company manufactures critical components used to move massive amounts of data at incredible speeds, exactly what AI systems require.

Coherent also announced a $50 million CHIPS Act grant, adding to previous state and local investments supporting the project.

Think about that for a minute...

✅ Billions in investment
✅ Thousands of jobs
✅ Major infrastructure projects
✅ Global technology companies betting on Sherman

This isn't just growth.

It's transformation.

📍 Keep your eye on Sherman. The rest of the country already is.

Ten years from now, people may look back and realize Sherman wasn't just growing, it was quietly becoming one of the biggest economic opportunities in North Texas.

text:
Many people ask why I am aggressively positioned across this specific supply chain: $SIVE, $AAOI, ShunSin, and FOCI.

​The biggest bottleneck in AI is no longer software, it’s physics. For the next generation of compute to function, datacenters must solve a critical equation:

1-Consume less power.

2-Generate less heat.

3- Multiply data bandwidth.

​Copper cables have hit their physical limits (too slow, too hot, too power-hungry). The only absolute structural solution is CPO (Co-Packaged Optics) and Silicon Photonics: replacing electricity with light.

​Here is why this exact basket of stocks is the literal chokepoint of this revolution:

​🔹 $SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors) - The Engine:

To have optics, you need a light source. Sivers manufactures the DFB laser arrays. Their specific architecture is the only one capable of providing the necessary power to hyperscalers while massively reducing the thermal footprint. It's the ignition.

​🔹 FOCI - The Optical Bridge:

Generating light is useless if you can't route it with surgical precision. FOCI is the leader in fiber arrays and micro-optic coupling. They connect this light to the silicon with zero signal loss.

​🔹 ShunSin - The Architect (Advanced Packaging):

Integrating lasers and fibers directly onto a chip requires atomic-level assembly technology (OSAT). ShunSin holds the advanced packaging expertise to assemble all these optical components as close to the AI processor as physically possible.

​🔹 $AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics) - The Deployment:

Once the chips are equipped, millions of servers need to be connected. AAOI mass-produces the ultra-high-speed optical transceivers and network infrastructure so the entire datacenter can communicate at the speed of light.

​While the retail herd chases chip designers at absurd valuations, smart money is quietly cornering the hardware bottleneck that is physically required for AI to scale by 2027.

​I don't trade hope. I trade physics. 🐺💡

#SiliconPhotonics #AI #CPO #Tech

text:
Identify the asymmetry in the rack and you'll find it in the stocks. 

Remember the insight that set off the memory supercycle? HBM per GPU went from 80GB on Hopper to a full TERABYTE on Rubin Ultra. 

Or how about when $INTC's CEO said the ratio of CPUs to GPUs would 8x?

Well the next asymmetric content step up in the data center is power chips.

NVIDIA's 2027 rack pulls 600,000 watts. That's 30x a normal server rack. At today's voltage the copper would melt. The only fix is going from 48V to 800V, and that forces every power chip in the rack to change.

Our modeling on power chip content per NVIDIA rack, Hopper to Rubin Ultra:

Power supply: $1,512 → $26,400 (17x)
GPU power board: $1,728 → $16,000 (9x)
Voltage regulators: $1,700 → $8,000 (5x)

GPUs had their run. CPUs had their run. Memory had its run. Photonics had its run. 

Power semis are just getting started. 

Just dropped the full breakdown on power semis: the physics explained for dummies, the three places inside the rack where the money goes, and the 15 name basket ranked by conviction. Link below or at bio.

text:
$AAOI up a good % today, and it's one of the most argued-about names on the photonics layer.

People love it or hate it. A 28-year-old Texas company that spent two decades as a sleepy cable TV supplier, now one of the most fought-over names in AI interconnect. 

Revenue more than doubled in a year.

I just did a deep dive, and the risk is real and so is the reward:
+They grow their own laser chips in-house while competitors wait in line for supply. The CEO calls it "complete backlog." That's the moat.

+Two customers = 82% of revenue. The single biggest risk and the single biggest strength, at the same time.

+The dilution story everyone gets wrong (it got cheaper, not worse).

+A 3.2T roadmap that is yet to be priced in.

AAOI is just how I like my stocks: High risk. High reward. 

The kind of name you size according to your risk tolerance and watch every earnings call.

Free Substack subscribers can redeem a one-time pass to read the full thing here:

https://t.co/vQBYY7sY8r

Bullish Photonics
